struct ShardFingerprint {\n69\t    /// Shard ID.\n70\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n71\t    /// Node ID.\n72\t    pub node_id: String,\n73\t    /// Merkle root of document hashes.\n74\t    pub merkle_root: result.\n82\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n83\tpub struct ReplicaDiff {\n84\t    /// Shard ID.\n85\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n86\t    /// Primary keys only in replica A.\n87\t    pub a_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n88\t    /// Primary keys only in replica B.\n89\t    pub b_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n90\t    /// Primary keys with content hash mismatch.\n91\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Repair action.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n96\tpub struct RepairAction {\n97\t    /// Shard ID.\n98\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n99\t    /// Primary key to repair.\n100\t    pub primary_key: String,\n101\t    /// Authoritative version (document JSON).\n102\t    pub authoritative_doc: serde_json::Value,\n103\t    /// Target nodes that need repair.\n104\t    pub target_nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n105\t    /// Reason for repair.\n106\t    pub reason: RepairReason,\n107\t}\n108\t\n109\t/// Why a repair is needed.\n110\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n111\tpub enum RepairReason {\n112\t    /// Document missing on replica.\n113\t    Missing,\n114\t    /// Content hash mismatch.\n115\t    Mismatch,\n116\t    /// Expired document resurrection (TTL interaction).\n117\t    ExpiredResurrection,\n118\t}\n119\t\n120\t/// Reconciler pass result.\n121\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n122\tpub struct ReconcilerPass {\n123\t    /// Pass started at (UNIX ms).\n124\t    pub started_at: u64,\n125\t    /// Pass completed at (UNIX ms).\n126\t    pub completed_at: repairs_performed: u32,\n133\t    /// Errors encountered.\n134\t    pub errors: Vec<String>,\n135\t}\n136\t\n137\t/// Anti-entropy reconciler.\n138\tpub struct AntiEntropyReconciler<C: NodeClient> {\n139\t    /// Configuration.\n140\t    config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n141\t    /// Shared topology.\n142\t    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n143\t    /// Pass history.\n144\t    pass_history: Arc<RwLock<Vec<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n145\t    /// Currently running pass.\n146\t    current_pass: Arc<RwLock<Option<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n147\t    /// HTTP client for node communication.\n148\t    node_client: Arc<C>,\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\timpl<C: NodeClient> AntiEntropyReconciler<C> {\n152\t    /// Create a new anti-entropy reconciler.\n153\t    pub fn new(\n154\t        config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n155\t        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n156\t        node_client: Arc<C>,\n157\t    ) -> Self {\n158\t        Self {\n159\t            config,\n160\t            topology,\n161\t            pass_history: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n162\t            current_pass: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n163\t            node_client,\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Compute bucket ID for a primary key (plan §13.8 step 2).\n168\t    ///\n169\t    /// Uses pk-hash modulo BUCKET_COUNT to isolate divergence to ~0.4% of PK space.\n170\t    /// This is reused by §13.1 reshard verify with PK-keyed bucketing.\n171\t    pub fn bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key: &str) -> usize {\n172\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n173\t        hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n174\t        (hasher.finish() as usize) % BUCKET_COUNT\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    /// Compute the canonical content hash of a document.\n178\t    ///\n179\t    /// The canonical form excludes internal Miroir fields (_miroir_*, _rankingScore)\n180\t    /// and serializes with sorted keys for deterministic hashing.\n181\t    pub fn compute_content_hash(document: &Value) -> u64 {\n182\t        // Remove internal fields to get canonical content\n183\t        let mut canonical = document.clone();\n184\t        if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object_mut() {\n185\t            // Remove all _miroir_* fields\n186\t            obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n187\t            // Remove _rankingScore (not content, used for scoring)\n188\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n189\t        }\n190\t\n191\t        // Serialize with sorted keys for deterministic output\n192\t        let canonical_json = if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object() {\n193\t            // Use BTreeMap to sort keys\n194\t            let sorted: BTreeMap<_, _> = obj.iter().collect();\n195\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n196\t        } else {\n197\t            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n198\t        };\n199\t\n200\t        // Hash using xxh3 (xxhash family, same as router)\n201\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n202\t        hasher.write(canonical_json.as_bytes());\n203\t        hasher.finish()\n204\t    }\n205\t\n206\t    /// Fingerprint a single shard on a node (plan §13.8 step 1).\n207\t    ///\n208\t    /// Iterates all documents with filter=_miroir_shard={id}, computes\n209\t    /// hash(primary_key || content_hash) for each, and folds into a\n210\t    /// streaming xxh3 digest. Also computes per-bucket hashes for diff.\n211\t    pub async fn fingerprint_shard(\n212\t        &self,\n213\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n214\t        shard_id: u32,\n215\t        index_uid: &str,\n216\t        address: &str,\n217\t    ) -> Result<ShardFingerprint> {\n218\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n219\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n220\t        let mut document_count = 0u64;\n221\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64); // Shard-seeded digest\n222\t\n223\t        // Per-bucket hashers for granular diff (plan §13.8 step 2)\n224\t        let mut bucket_hashers: Vec<XxHash64> = (0..BUCKET_COUNT)\n225\t            .map(|_| XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64))\n226\t            .collect();\n227\t\n228\t        // Paginated iteration through documents\n229\t        loop {\n230\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n231\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n232\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n233\t                filter,\n234\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n235\t                offset,\n236\t            };\n237\t\n238\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n239\t                .node_client\n240\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n241\t                .await\n242\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n243\t\n244\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n245\t                break; // No more documents\n246\t            }\n247\t\n248\t            for doc in &response.results {\n249\t                // Extract primary key\n250\t                let primary_key = doc\n251\t                    .get(\"id\")\n252\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n253\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n254\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n255\t\n256\t                // Compute content hash\n257\t                let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n258\t\n259\t                // Fold: hash(primary_key || content_hash) into digest\n260\t                let mut pk_hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n261\t                pk_hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n262\t                pk_hasher.write_u64(content_hash);\n263\t                let doc_hash = pk_hasher.finish();\n264\t\n265\t                // Fold into shard digest\n266\t                hasher.write_u64(doc_hash);\n267\t                document_count += 1;\n268\t\n269\t                // Fold into bucket digest for granular diff\n270\t                let bucket_id = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n271\t                bucket_hashers[bucket_id].write_u64(doc_hash);\n272\t            }\n273\t\n274\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n275\t\n276\t            // Self-throttle: small sleep between batches to target <2% CPU\n277\t            // (In production, this would be adaptive based on CPU metrics)\n278\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n279\t        }\n280\t\n281\t        let merkle_root = format!(\"xxh3:{}\", hasher.finish());\n282\t\n283\t        // Extract per-bucket hashes\n284\t        let bucket_hashes: Vec<String> = bucket_hashers\n285\t            .into_iter()\n286\t            .map(|h| format!(\"xxh3:{}\", h.finish()))\n287\t            .collect();\n288\t\n289\t        debug!(\n290\t            \"Fingerprinted shard {} on node {}: {} documents, root {}\",\n291\t            shard_id, node_id, document_count, merkle_root\n292\t        );\n293\t\n294\t        Ok(ShardFingerprint {\n295\t            shard_id,\n296\t            node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n297\t            merkle_root,\n298\t            document_count,\n299\t            bucket_hashes,\n300\t        })\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    /// Find divergent buckets between two fingerprints (plan §13.8 step 2).\n304\t    ///\n305\t    /// Returns bucket IDs where the per-bucket hashes differ.\n306\t    /// Each bucket isolates divergence to ~0.4% of the PK space.\n307\t    pub fn diff_fingerprints(\n308\t        &self,\n309\t        fp_a: &ShardFingerprint,\n310\t        fp_b: &ShardFingerprint,\n311\t    ) -> Vec<usize> {\n312\t        let mut divergent = Vec::new();\n313\t\n314\t        if fp_a.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT || fp_b.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT {\n315\t            // Fallback: if bucket hashes aren't computed, treat all buckets as divergent\n316\t            warn!(\n317\t                \"Bucket hashes not computed, treating all {} buckets as divergent\",\n318\t                BUCKET_COUNT\n319\t            );\n320\t            return (0..BUCKET_COUNT).collect();\n321\t        }\n322\t\n323\t        for (bucket_id, (hash_a, hash_b)) in fp_a\n324\t            .bucket_hashes\n325\t            .iter()\n326\t            .zip(fp_b.bucket_hashes.iter())\n327\t            .enumerate()\n328\t        {\n329\t            if hash_a != hash_b {\n330\t                divergent.push(bucket_id);\n331\t            }\n332\t        }\n333\t\n334\t        divergent\n335\t    }\n336\t\n337\t    /// Fetch all primary keys in a specific bucket (plan §13.8 step 2).\n338\t    ///\n339\t    /// Returns a map of primary key to content hash for all documents\n340\t    /// in the given bucket on the specified replica.\n341\t    pub async fn fetch_bucket_pks(\n342\t        &self,\n343\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n344\t        shard_id: u32,\n345\t        bucket_id: usize,\n346\t        index_uid: &str,\n347\t        address: &str,\n348\t    ) -> Result<HashMap<String, u64>> {\n349\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n350\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n351\t        let mut bucket_pks = HashMap::new();\n352\t\n353\t        loop {\n354\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n355\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n356\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n357\t                filter,\n358\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n359\t                offset,\n360\t            };\n361\t\n362\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n363\t                .node_client\n364\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n365\t                .await\n366\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n367\t\n368\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n369\t                break;\n370\t            }\n371\t\n372\t            for doc in &response.results {\n373\t                let primary_key = doc\n374\t                    .get(\"id\")\n375\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n376\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n377\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n378\t\n379\t                // Check if this document belongs to the target bucket\n380\t                let doc_bucket = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n381\t                if doc_bucket == bucket_id {\n382\t                    let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n383\t                    bucket_pks.insert(primary_key.to_string(), content_hash);\n384\t                }\n385\t            }\n386\t\n387\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n388\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n389\t        }\n390\t\n391\t        Ok(bucket_pks)\n392\t    }\n393\t\n394\t    /// Compare replicas within a divergent bucket to find specific PK differences.\n395\t    ///\n396\t    /// Returns a ReplicaDiff listing:\n397\t    /// - PKs only in replica A\n398\t    /// - PKs only in replica B\n399\t    /// - PKs with different content hashes\n400\t    pub async fn compare_bucket_replicas(\n401\t        &self,\n402\t        shard_id: u32,\n403\t        bucket_id: usize,\n404\t        node_a: &NodeId,\n405\t        address_a: &str,\n406\t        node_b: &NodeId,\n407\t        address_b: &str,\n408\t        index_uid: &str,\n409\t    ) -> Result<ReplicaDiff> {\n410\t        let pks_a = self\n411\t            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_a, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_a)\n412\t            .await?;\n413\t        let pks_b = self\n414\t            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_b, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_b)\n415\t            .await?;\n416\t\n417\t        let mut a_only = Vec::new();\n418\t        let mut b_only = Vec::new();\n419\t        let mut mismatched = Vec::new();\n420\t\n421\t        // Find PKs only in A or with mismatched content\n422\t        for (pk, hash_a) in &pks_a {\n423\t            match pks_b.get(pk) {\n424\t                Some(hash_b) if hash_b != hash_a => {\n425\t                    mismatched.push(pk.clone());\n426\t                }\n427\t                None => {\n428\t                    a_only.push(pk.clone());\n429\t                }\n430\t                _ => {}\n431\t            }\n432\t        }\n433\t\n434\t        // Find PKs only in B\n435\t        for pk in pks_b.keys() {\n436\t            if !pks_a.contains_key(pk) {\n437\t                b_only.push(pk.clone());\n438\t            }\n439\t        }\n440\t\n441\t        Ok(ReplicaDiff {\n442\t            shard_id,\n443\t            a_only_pks: a_only,\n444\t            b_only_pks: b_only,\n445\t            mismatched_pks: mismatched,\n446\t        })\n447\t    }\n448\t\n449\t    /// Run a single reconciliation pass.\n450\t    pub async fn run_pass(&self) -> Result<ReconcilerPass> {\n451\t        let mut pass = ReconcilerPass {\n452\t            started_at: millis_now(),\n453\t            completed_at: 0,\n454\t            shards_scanned: 0,\n455\t            shards_with_drift: 0,\n456\t            repairs_performed: 0,\n457\t            errors: Vec::new(),\n458\t        };\n459\t\n460\t        // Set as current pass\n461\t        {\n462\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n463\t            *current = Some(pass.clone());\n464\t        }\n465\t\n466\t        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n467\t        let shard_count = topology.shards;\n468\t        let replica_groups = topology.groups().count() as u32;\n469\t\n470\t        // Determine which shards to scan\n471\t        let shards_to_scan = if self.config.shards_per_pass == 0 {\n472\t            // Scan all shards\n473\t            (0..shard_count).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n474\t        } else {\n475\t            // Scan a subset (for throttling)\n476\t            (0..shard_count)\n477\t                .take(self.config.shards_per_pass as usize)\n478\t                .collect()\n479\t        };\n480\t\n481\t        info!(\n482\t            \"Anti-entropy pass starting: {} shards to scan\",\n483\t            shards_to_scan.len()\n484\t        );\n485\t\n486\t        // Scan each shard\n487\t        for shard_id in shards_to_scan {\n488\t            match self.scan_shard(&topology, shard_id).await {\n489\t                Ok(drift_detected) => {\n490\t                    pass.shards_scanned += 1;\n491\t                    if drift_detected {\n492\t                        pass.shards_with_drift += 1;\n493\t                    }\n494\t                }\n495\t                Err(e) => {\n496\t                    pass.errors.push(format!(\"shard {}: {}\", shard_id, e));\n497\t                }\n498\t            }\n499\t        }\n500\t\n501\t        pass.completed_at = millis_now();\n502\t\n503\t        // Archive pass\n504\t        {\n505\t            let mut history = self.pass_history.write().await;\n506\t            history.push(pass.clone());\n507\t            // Keep last 100 passes\n508\t            if history.len() > 100 {\n509\t                history.remove(0);\n510\t            }\n511\t        }\n512\t\n513\t        // Clear current pass\n514\t        {\n515\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n516\t            *current = None;\n517\t        }\n518\t\n519\t        info!(\n520\t            \"Anti-entropy pass completed: {} shards scanned, {} with drift, {} repairs\",\n521\t            pass.shards_scanned, pass.shards_with_drift, pass.repairs_performed\n522\t        );\n523\t\n524\t        Ok(pass)\n525\t    }\n526\t\n527\t    /// Scan a single shard for drift.\n528\t    async fn scan_shard(&self, topology: &Topology, shard_id: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n529\t        // For each replica group, get the assigned nodes\n530\t        let mut fingerprints = Vec::new();\n531\t\n532\t        for group in topology.groups() {\n533\t            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n534\t            for node_id in assigned {\n535\t                // Look up node address from topology\n536\t                let topology_guard = self.topology.read().await;\n537\t                let node = topology_guard\n538\t                    .node(&node_id)\n539\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n540\t\n541\t                if !node.is_healthy() {\n542\t                    warn!(\"Node {} is not healthy, skipping fingerprint\", node_id);\n543\t                    continue;\n544\t                }\n545\t\n546\t                let address = node.address.clone();\n547\t                drop(topology_guard);\n548\t\n549\t                match self\n550\t                    .fingerprint_shard(&node_id, shard_id, &self.config.index_uid, &address)\n551\t                    .await\n552\t                {\n553\t                    Ok(fp) => fingerprints.push((node_id.clone(), address, fp)),\n554\t                    Err(e) => {\n555\t                        warn!(\n556\t                            \"Failed to fingerprint shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n557\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n558\t                        );\n559\t                        // Continue with other nodes\n560\t                    }\n561\t                }\n562\t            }\n563\t        }\n564\t\n565\t        if fingerprints.is_empty() {\n566\t            // No readable replicas\n567\t            return Ok(false);\n568\t        }\n569\t\n570\t        // Compare fingerprints\n571\t        let (ref_node_id, ref_address, reference) = &fingerprints[0];\n572\t        let mut drift_detected = false;\n573\t\n574\t        for (node_id, address, fp) in &fingerprints[1..] {\n575\t            if fp.merkle_root != reference.merkle_root {\n576\t                drift_detected = true;\n577\t                debug!(\n578\t                    \"Shard {} drift detected: node {} has different merkle root\",\n579\t                    shard_id, node_id\n580\t                );\n581\t\n582\t                if self.config.auto_repair {\n583\t                    // Perform detailed diff and repair\n584\t                    if let Err(e) = self\n585\t                        .repair_shard(\n586\t                            shard_id,\n587\t                            ref_node_id,\n588\t                            ref_address,\n589\t                            reference,\n590\t                            node_id,\n591\t                            address,\n592\t                            fp,\n593\t                        )\n594\t                        .await\n595\t                    {\n596\t                        error!(\n597\t                            \"Failed to repair shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n598\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n599\t                        );\n600\t                    }\n601\t                }\n602\t            }\n603\t        }\n604\t\n605\t        Ok(drift_detected)\n606\t    }\n607\t\n608\t    /// Repair a shard by comparing replicas and applying fixes (plan §13.8 step 3).\n609\t    async fn repair_shard(\n610\t        &self,\n611\t        shard_id: u32,\n612\t        reference_node: &NodeId,\n613\t        reference_address: &str,\n614\t        reference_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n615\t        target_node: &NodeId,\n616\t        target_address: &str,\n617\t        target_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n618\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n619\t        debug!(\n620\t            \"Repairing shard {} on node {} (reference: node {})\",\n621\t            shard_id, target_node, reference_node\n622\t        );\n623\t\n624\t        // Step 1: Find divergent buckets using per-bucket hashes (plan §13.8 step 2)\n625\t        let divergent_buckets = self.diff_fingerprints(reference_fp, target_fp);\n626\t\n627\t        if divergent_buckets.is_empty() {\n628\t            // No bucket-level divergence (shouldn't happen if merkle roots differ)\n629\t            warn!(\n630\t                \"Shard {} merkle roots differ but no bucket divergence found\",\n631\t                shard_id\n632\t            );\n633\t            return Ok(());\n634\t        }\n635\t\n636\t        debug!(\n637\t            \"Shard {} has {} divergent buckets out of {}\",\n638\t            shard_id,\n639\t            divergent_buckets.len(),\n640\t            BUCKET_COUNT\n641\t        );\n642\t\n643\t        // Step 2: For each divergent bucket, enumerate divergent PKs\n644\t        for bucket_id in divergent_buckets {\n645\t            match self\n646\t                .compare_bucket_replicas(\n647\t                    shard_id,\n648\t                    bucket_id,\n649\t                    reference_node,\n650\t                    reference_address,\n651\t                    target_node,\n652\t                    target_address,\n653\t                    &self.config.index_uid,\n654\t                )\n655\t                .await\n656\t            {\n657\t                Ok(diff) => {\n658\t                    let total_divergent =\n659\t                        diff.a_only_pks.len() + diff.b_only_pks.len() + diff.mismatched_pks.len();\n660\t\n661\t                    if total_divergent > 0 {\n662\t                        debug!(\n663\t                            \"Bucket {} in shard {}: {} divergent PKs ({} A-only, {} B-only, {} mismatched)\",\n664\t                            bucket_id,\n665\t                            shard_id,\n666\t                            total_divergent,\n667\t                            diff.a_only_pks.len(),\n668\t                            diff.b_only_pks.len(),\n669\t                            diff.mismatched_pks.len()\n670\t                        );\n671\t\n672\t                        // Step 3: For each divergent PK, apply repair (plan §13.8 step 3)\n673\t                        // TODO: Implement actual repair writes\n674\t                        // For now, just log the divergence\n675\t                        for pk in &diff.a_only_pks {\n676\t                            debug!(\"PK {} only exists on reference node {}\", pk, reference_node);\n677\t                        }\n678\t                        for pk in &diff.b_only_pks {\n679\t                            debug!(\"PK {} only exists on target node {}\", pk, target_node);\n680\t                        }\n681\t                        for pk in &diff.mismatched_pks {\n682\t                            debug!(\"PK {} has mismatched content\", pk);\n683\t                        }\n684\t                    }\n685\t                }\n686\t                Err(e) => {\n687\t                    warn!(\n688\t                        \"Failed to compare bucket {} in shard {}: {}\",\n689\t                        bucket_id, shard_id, e\n690\t                    );\n691\t                }\n692\t            }\n693\t        }\n694\t\n695\t        Ok(())\n696\t    }\n697\t\n698\t    /// Get pass history.\n699\t    pub async fn pass_history(&self) -> Vec<ReconcilerPass> {\n700\t        self.pass_history.read().await.clone()\n701\t    }\n702\t\n703\t    /// Get current pass in progress.\n704\t    pub async fn current_pass(&self) -> Option<ReconcilerPass> {\n705\t        self.current_pass.read().await.clone()\n706\t    }\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n710\t/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n711\t///\n712\t/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n713\t/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n714\t/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n715\t/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\n716\tpub fn validate_migration_safety(\n717\t    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n718\t    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n719\t) -> std::result::Result<(), MigrationError> {\n720\t    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n721\t        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n722\t    }\n723\t    Ok(())\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n727\t/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n728\t///\n729\t/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n730\t/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n731\t/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\n732\tpub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n733\t    if ae_enabled {\n734\t        return None;\n735\t    }\n736\t    Some(\n737\t        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n738\t         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n739\t         data loss at the cutover boundary. Re-enable anti-entropy for defense-in-depth.\"\n740\t            .to_string(),\n741\t    )\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n745\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n746\t    SystemTime::now()\n747\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n748\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n749\t        .as_millis() as u64\n750\t}\n751\t\n752\t#[cfg(test)]\n753\tmod tests {\n754\t    use };\n767\t        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_ok());\n768\t    }\n769\t\n770\t    #[test]\n771\t    fn test_validate_unsafe_without_anti_entropy() {\n772\t        let ae = Supports formats like \"every 6h\", \"every 30m\", \"every 1h\".\n49\t    /// Returns interval in seconds, or 21600 (6h) if parsing fails.\n50\t    pub fn from_schedule(schedule: &str) -> Self {\n51\t        let interval_s = parse_schedule_interval(schedule).unwrap_or(6 * 3600);\n52\t        Self {\n53\t            interval_s,\n54\t            ..Default::default()\n55\t        }\n56\t    }\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\t/// Parse schedule interval string to seconds.\n60\t///\n61\t/// Examples:\n62\t/// - \"every 6h\" -> 21600\n63\t/// - \"every 30m\" -> 1800\n64\t/// - \"every 1h\" -> 3600\n65\tfn parse_schedule_interval(schedule: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n66\t    let schedule = schedule.trim().to_lowercase();\n67\t\n68\t    // Match \"every X[unit]\" pattern\n69\t    if !schedule.starts_with(\"every \") {\n70\t        return None;\n71\t    }\n72\t\n73\t    let rest = schedule[6..].trim();\n74\t    if rest.is_empty() {\n75\t        return None;\n76\t    }\n77\t\n78\t    // Find the first non-digit character to split number from unit\n79\t    let mut num_end = 0;\n80\t    for (i, c) in rest.chars().enumerate() {\n81\t        if !c.is_ascii_digit() {\n82\t            num_end = i;\n83\t            break;\n84\t        }\n85\t    }\n86\t\n87\t    if num_end == 0 {\n88\t        return None;\n89\t    }\n90\t\n91\t    let num_str = &rest[..num_end];\n92\t    let unit = &rest[num_end..];\n93\t\n94\t    let value: u64 = num_str.parse().ok()?;\n95\t\n96\t    match unit {\n97\t        \"s\" | \"sec\" | \"second\" | \"seconds\" => Some(value),\n98\t        \"m\" | \"min\" | \"minute\" | \"minutes\" => Some(value * 60),\n99\t        \"h\" | \"hour\" | \"hours\" => Some(value * 3600),\n100\t        _ => None,\n101\t    }\n102\t}\n103\t\n104\t/// HTTP-based node client for anti-entropy fingerprinting.\n105\t///\n106\t/// Implements the NodeClient trait for fetching documents from Meilisearch nodes\n107\t/// during anti-entropy                request.limit,\n155\t                request.offset\n156\t            )\n157\t        };\n158\t\n159\t        let response = self.client\n160\t            .get(&url)\n161\t       .json()\n174\t            .await\n175\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"parse failed: {}\", e)))?;\n176\t\n177\t        let results = json\n178\t            .get(\"results\")\n179\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n180\t            .map(|v| v.clone())\n181\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n182\t\n183\t        let total = json\n184\t            .get(\"total\")\n185\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n186\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n187\t\n188\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n189\t            results,\n190\t            limit:         .unwrap_or(0);\n262\t\n263\t        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n264\t            total_docs,\n265\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n266\t            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n267\t        })\n268\t    }\n269\t}\n270\t\n271\t/// Anti-entropy background worker.\n272\t///\n273\t/// Runs periodic anti-entropy passes with leader election to ensure\n274\t/// only one pod runs the fingerprinting at a time.\n275\tpub struct AntiEntropyWorker {\n276\t    config: AntiEntropyWorkerConfig,\n277\t    reconciler: AntiEntropyReconciler<HttpNodeClient>,\n278\t    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n279\t    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n280\t    pod_id: String,\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\timpl AntiEntropyWorker {\n284\t    /// Create a new anti-entropy worker.\n285\t    pub fn new(\n286\t        config: AntiEntropyWorkerConfig,\n287\t        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n288\t        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n289\t        node_master_key: String,\n290\t        pod_id: String,\n291\t    ) -> Self {\n292\t        let ae_config = AntiEntropyConfig {\n293\t            enabled: true,\n294\t            schedule: format!(\"every {}s\", config.interval_s),\n295\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n296\t            shards_per_pass: 0, // Scan all shards\n297\t            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n298\t            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n299\t            auto_repair: true,\n300\t            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n301\t        };\n302\t\n303\t        let node_client = HttpNodeClient::new(node_master_key);\n304\t        let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::new(\n305\t            ae_config,\n306\t            topology.clone(),\n307\t            Arc::new(node_client),\n308\t        );\n309\t\n310\t        Self {\n311\t            config,\n312\t            reconciler,\n313\t            topology,\n314\t            task_store,\n315\t            pod_id,\n316\t        }\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    /// Start the background worker.\n320\t    ///\n321\t    /// This runs in a loop:\n322\t    /// 1. Try to acquire leader lease (scope: anti_entropy)\n323\t    /// 2. If acquired, run anti-entropy pass\n324\t    /// 3. Renew lease periodically\n325\t    /// 4. If lease lost, go back to step 1\n326\t    pub async fn run(&self) {\n327\t        info!(\n328\t            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n329\t            interval_s = self.config.interval_s,\n330\t            \"anti-entropy worker starting\"\n331\t        );\n332\t\n333\t        let scope = \"anti_entropy\";\n334\t\n335\t        loop {\n336\t            let now_ms = now_ms();\n337\t            let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n338\t\n339\t            // Try to acquire leader lease\n340\t            match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n341\t                let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n342\t                let scope = scope.to_string();\n343\t                let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n344\t                move || {\n345\t                    task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at, now_ms)\n346\t                }\n347\t            })\n348\t            .await\n349\t            {\n350\t                Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n351\t                    info!(scope = %scope, pod_id = %self.pod_id, \"acquired leader lease\");\n352\t\n353\t                    // We are the leader - run anti-entropy pass cycle\n354\t                    if let Err(e) = self.run_pass_cycle().await {\n355\t                        error!(error = %e, \"anti-entropy pass cycle failed\");\n356\t                    }\n357\t                }\n358\t                Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n359\t                    debug!(scope = %scope, \"leader lease already held\");\n360\t                }\n361\t                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n362\t                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to acquire leader lease\");\n363\t                }\n364\t                Err(e) => {\n365\t                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n366\t                }\n367\t            }\n368\t\n369\t            // Wait before retrying lease acquisition\n370\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n371\t                self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n372\t            ))\n373\t            .await;\n374\t        }\n375\t    }\n376\t\n377\t    /// Run a single anti-entropy pass cycle.\n378\t    ///\n379\t    /// This runs the pass immediately after acquiring lease, then waits\n380\t    /// for the configured interval before running again (if still leader).\n381\t    async fn run_pass_cycle(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n382\t        let scope = \"anti_entropy\";\n383\t        let mut lease_renewal = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n384\t            self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n385\t        ));\n386\t\n387\t        // Run anti-entropy pass immediately on acquiring lease\n388\t        self.run_single_pass().await?;\n389\t\n390\t        // Then wait for interval or lease expiry\n391\t        let pass_interval = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(self.config.interval_s));\n392\t\n393\t        tokio::select! {\n394\t            _ = lease_renewal.tick() => {\n395\t                // Renew lease\n396\t                let now_ms = now_ms();\n397\t                let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n398\t\n399\t                match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n400\t                    let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n401\t                    let scope = scope.to_string();\n402\t                    let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n403\t                    move || {\n404\t                        task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at)\n405\t                    }\n406\t                })\n407\t                .await\n408\t                {\n409\t                    Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n410\t                        debug!(scope = %scope, \"renewed leader lease\");\n411\t                    }\n412\t                    Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n413\t                        info!(scope = %scope, \"lost leader lease\");\n414\t                        return Ok(());\n415\t                    }\n416\t                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n417\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to renew leader lease\");\n418\t                        return Err(e.to_string());\n419\t                    }\n420\t                    Err(e) => {\n421\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n422\t                        return Err(format!(\"spawn_blocking task failed: {}\", e));\n423\t                    }\n424\t                }\n425\t            }\n426\t            _ = pass_interval => {\n427\t                // Interval passed - run anti-entropy pass\n428\t                self.run_single_pass().await?;\n429\t            }\n430\t        }\n431\t\n432\t        Ok(())\n433\t    }\n434\t\n435\t    /// Run a single anti-entropy pass.\n436\t    async fn run_single_pass(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n437\t        info!(\"starting anti-entropy pass\");\n438\t\n439\t        match self.reconciler.run_pass().await {\n440\t            Ok(pass) => {\n441\t                info!(\n442\t                    shards_scanned = pass.shards_scanned,\n443\t                    shards_with_drift = pass.shards_with_drift,\n444\t                    repairs_performed = pass.repairs_performed,\n445\t                    errors = pass.errors.len(),\n446\t                    \"anti-entropy pass completed\"\n447\t                );\n448\t\n449\t                if !pass.errors.is_empty() {\n450\t                    warn!(errors = ?pass.errors, \"anti-entropy pass had errors\");\n451\t                }\n452\t\n453\t                Ok(())\n454\t            }\n455\t            Err(e) => {\n456\t                Err(format!(\"anti-entropy pass failed: {}\", e))\n457\t            }\n458\t        }\n459\t    }\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\t/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\n463\tfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n464\t    SystemTime::now()\n465\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n466\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n467\t        .as_millis() as );\n482\t\n483\t    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n484\t    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n485\t        shard_id: 0,\n486\t        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n487\t let mut fp_b = ShardFingerprint {\n493\t        shard_id: 0,\n494\t        node_id: \"node-b\".to_string(),\n495\t        merkle_root: \"xxh3:456\".to_string(),\n496\t        document_count: 10000,\n497\t        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n498\t    };\n499\t\n500\t    // Make 100 buckets divergent\n501\t    for i in 0..100 {\n502\t        fp_b.bucket_hashes[i] = format!(\"xxh3:divergent-{}\", i);\n503\t    }\n504\t\n505\t    let divergent = reconciler.diff_fingerprints(&fp_a, &fp_b);\n506\t\n507\t    assert_eq!(divergent.len(), 100);\n508\t\n509\t    // Each divergent bucket represents ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of PK space\n510\t    // 100 buckets ≈ 39% of total PK space\n511\t    let isolation_ratio = divergent.len() as f64 / BUCKET_COUNT as f64;\n512\t    assert!((isolation_ratio - (100.0 / 256.0)).abs() < 0.01);\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t#[tokio::test]\n516\tasync fn test_bucket_count_constant() {\n517\t    // Verify 256);\n519\t}\n520\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_7e034afb6de5418d9f91a953","session_id":"336f80db-0374-48ee-8311-5bb3f034a38d","uuid":"75e23714-8d90-4a28-a8a9-0854bb310ffc","timestamp":"2026-05-23T12:41:57.525Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_7e034afb6de5418d9f91a953","session_id":"336f80db-0374-48ee-8311-5bb3f034a38d","uuid":"5c511892-9e57-46ac-9c1a-14301c5e240d","timestamp":"2026-05-23T12:42:09.818Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore anti-entropy codebase"}

test_diff_fingerprints_isolates_divergence() {\n    // Test that divergent buckets isolate to ~0.4% of PK space\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockTestNodeClient>::new(\n        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n        topology,\n        Arc::new(MockTestNodeClient::new()),\n    );\n\n    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:123\".to_string(),\n        document_count: Default for AntiEntropyConfig {\n52\t    fn default() -> Self {\n53\t        Self {\n54\t            enabled: true,\n55\t            schedule: \"every 6h\".to_string(),\n56\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n57\t            shards_per_pass: 0,\n58\t            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n59\t            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n60\t            auto_repair: true,\n61\t            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n62\t        }\n63\t    }\n64\t}\n65\t\n66\t/// Shard fingerprint for comparison.\n67\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n68\tpub struct ShardFingerprint {\n69\t    /// Shard ID.\n70\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n71\t    /// Node ID.\n72\t    pub node_id: String,\n73\t    /// Merkle root of document hashes.\n74\t    pub merkle_root: String,\n75\t    /// Document count.\n76\t    pub document_count: u64,\n77\t    /// Per-bucket hashes for detailed diff.\n78\t    pub bucket_hashes: Vec<String>,\n79\t}\n80\t\n81\t/// Replica diff result.\n82\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n83\tpub struct ReplicaDiff {\n84\t    /// Shard ID.\n85\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n86\t    /// Primary keys only in replica A.\n87\t    pub a_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n88\t    /// Primary keys only in replica B.\n89\t    pub b_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n90\t    /// Primary keys with content hash mismatch.\n91\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Repair action.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n96\tpub struct RepairAction {\n97\t    /// Shard ID.\n98\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n99\t    /// Primary key to repair.\n100\t    pub primary_key: String,\n101\t    /// Authoritative version (document JSON).\n102\t    pub authoritative_doc: serde_json::Value,\n103\t    /// Target nodes that need repair.\n104\t    pub target_nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n105\t    /// Reason for repair.\n106\t    pub reason: RepairReason,\n107\t}\n108\t\n109\t/// Why a repair is needed.\n110\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n111\tpub enum RepairReason {\n112\t    /// Document missing on replica.\n113\t    Missing,\n114\t    /// Content hash mismatch.\n115\t    Mismatch,\n116\t    /// Expired document resurrection (TTL interaction).\n117\t    ExpiredResurrection,\n118\t}\n119\t\n120\t/// Reconciler pass result.\n121\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n122\tpub struct ReconcilerPass {\n123\t    /// Pass started at (UNIX ms).\n124\t    pub started_at: u64,\n125\t    /// Pass completed at (UNIX ms).\n126\t    pub completed_at: u64,\n127\t    /// Shards scanned.\n128\t    pub shards_scanned: u32,\n129\t    /// Shards with drift detected.\n130\t    pub shards_with_drift: u32,\n131\t    /// Repairs performed.\n132\t    pub repairs_performed: u32,\n133\t    /// Errors encountered.\n134\t    pub errors: Vec<String>,\n135\t}\n136\t\n137\t/// Anti-entropy reconciler.\n138\tpub struct AntiEntropyReconciler<C: NodeClient> {\n139\t    /// Configuration.\n140\t    config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n141\t    /// Shared topology.\n142\t    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n143\t    /// Pass history.\n144\t    pass_history: Arc<RwLock<Vec<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n145\t    /// Currently running pass.\n146\t    current_pass: Arc<RwLock<Option<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n147\t    /// HTTP client for node communication.\n148\t    node_client: Arc<C>,\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\timpl<C: NodeClient> AntiEntropyReconciler<C> {\n152\t    /// Create a new anti-entropy reconciler.\n153\t    pub fn new(\n154\t        config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n155\t        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n156\t        node_client: Arc<C>,\n157\t    ) -> Self {\n158\t        Self {\n159\t            config,\n160\t            topology,\n161\t            pass_history: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n162\t            current_pass: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n163\t            node_client,\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Compute bucket ID for a primary key (plan §13.8 step 2).\n168\t    ///\n169\t    /// Uses pk-hash modulo BUCKET_COUNT to isolate divergence to ~0.4% of PK space.\n170\t    /// This is reused by §13.1 reshard verify with PK-keyed bucketing.\n171\t    pub fn bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key: &str) -> usize {\n172\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n173\t        hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n174\t        (hasher.finish() as usize) % BUCKET_COUNT\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    /// Compute the canonical content hash of a document.\n178\t    ///\n179\t    /// The canonical form excludes internal Miroir fields (_miroir_*, _rankingScore)\n180\t    /// and serializes with sorted keys for deterministic hashing.\n181\t    pub fn compute_content_hash(document: &Value) -> u64 {\n182\t        // Remove internal fields to get canonical content\n183\t        let mut canonical = document.clone();\n184\t        if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object_mut() {\n185\t            // Remove all _miroir_* fields\n186\t            obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n187\t            // Remove _rankingScore (not content, used for scoring)\n188\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n189\t        }\n190\t\n191\t        // Serialize with sorted keys for deterministic output\n192\t        let canonical_json = if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object() {\n193\t            // Use BTreeMap to sort keys\n194\t            let sorted: BTreeMap<_, _> = obj.iter().collect();\n195\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n196\t        } else {\n197\t            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n198\t        };\n199\t\n200\t        // Hash using xxh3 (xxhash family, same as router)\n201\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n202\t        hasher.write(canonical_json.as_bytes());\n203\t        hasher.finish()\n204\t    }\n205\t\n206\t    /// Fingerprint a single shard on a node (plan §13.8 step 1).\n207\t    ///\n208\t    /// Iterates all documents with filter=_miroir_shard={id}, computes\n209\t    /// hash(primary_key || content_hash) for each, and folds into a\n210\t    /// streaming xxh3 digest. Also computes per-bucket hashes for diff.\n211\t    pub async fn fingerprint_shard(\n212\t        &self,\n213\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n214\t        shard_id: u32,\n215\t        index_uid: &str,\n216\t        address: &str,\n217\t    ) -> Result<ShardFingerprint> {\n218\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n219\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n220\t        let mut document_count = 0u64;\n221\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64); // Shard-seeded digest\n222\t\n223\t        // Per-bucket hashers for granular diff (plan §13.8 step 2)\n224\t        let mut bucket_hashers: Vec<XxHash64> = (0..BUCKET_COUNT)\n225\t            .map(|_| XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64))\n226\t            .collect();\n227\t\n228\t        // Paginated iteration through documents\n229\t        loop {\n230\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n231\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n232\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n233\t                filter,\n234\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n235\t                offset,\n236\t            };\n237\t\n238\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n239\t                .node_client\n240\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n241\t                .await\n242\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n243\t\n244\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n245\t                break; // No more documents\n246\t            }\n247\t\n248\t            for doc in &response.results {\n249\t                // Extract primary key\n250\t                let primary_key = doc\n251\t                    .get(\"id\")\n252\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n253\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n254\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n255\t\n256\t                // Compute content hash\n257\t                let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n258\t\n259\t                // Fold: hash(primary_key || content_hash) into digest\n260\t                let mut pk_hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n261\t                pk_hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n262\t                pk_hasher.write_u64(content_hash);\n263\t                let doc_hash = pk_hasher.finish();\n264\t\n265\t                // Fold into shard digest\n266\t                hasher.write_u64(doc_hash);\n267\t                document_count += 1;\n268\t\n269\t                // Fold into bucket digest for granular diff\n270\t                let bucket_id = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n271\t                bucket_hashers[bucket_id].write_u64(doc_hash);\n272\t            }\n273\t\n274\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n275\t\n276\t            // Self-throttle: small sleep between batches to target <2% CPU\n277\t            // (In production, this would be adaptive based on CPU metrics)\n278\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n279\t        }\n280\t\n281\t        let merkle_root = format!(\"xxh3:{}\", hasher.finish());\n282\t\n283\t        // Extract per-bucket hashes\n284\t        let bucket_hashes: Vec<String> = bucket_hashers\n285\t            .into_iter()\n286\t            .map(|h| format!(\"xxh3:{}\", h.finish()))\n287\t            .collect();\n288\t\n289\t        debug!(\n290\t            \"Fingerprinted shard {} on node {}: {} documents, root {}\",\n291\t            shard_id, node_id, document_count, merkle_root\n292\t        );\n293\t\n294\t        Ok(ShardFingerprint {\n295\t            shard_id,\n296\t            node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n297\t            merkle_root,\n298\t            document_count,\n299\t            bucket_hashes,\n300\t        })\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    /// Find divergent buckets between two fingerprints (plan §13.8 step 2).\n304\t    ///\n305\t    /// Returns bucket IDs where the per-bucket hashes differ.\n306\t    /// Each bucket isolates divergence to ~0.4% of the PK space.\n307\t    pub fn diff_fingerprints(\n308\t        &self,\n309\t        fp_a: &ShardFingerprint,\n310\t        fp_b: &ShardFingerprint,\n311\t    ) -> Vec<usize> {\n312\t        let mut divergent = Vec::new();\n313\t\n314\t        if fp_a.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT || fp_b.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT {\n315\t            // Fallback: if bucket hashes aren't computed, treat all buckets as divergent\n316\t            warn!(\n317\t                \"Bucket hashes not computed, treating all {} buckets as divergent\",\n318\t                BUCKET_COUNT\n319\t            );\n320\t            return (0..BUCKET_COUNT).collect();\n321\t        }\n322\t\n323\t        for (bucket_id, (hash_a, hash_b)) in fp_a\n324\t            .bucket_hashes\n325\t            .iter()\n326\t            .zip(fp_b.bucket_hashes.iter())\n327\t            .enumerate()\n328\t        {\n329\t            if hash_a != hash_b {\n330\t                divergent.push(bucket_id);\n331\t            }\n332\t        }\n333\t\n334\t        divergent\n335\t    }\n336\t\n337\t    /// Fetch all primary keys in a specific bucket (plan §13.8 step 2).\n338\t    ///\n339\t    /// Returns a map of primary key to content hash for all documents\n340\t    /// in the given bucket on the specified replica.\n341\t    pub async fn fetch_bucket_pks(\n342\t        &self,\n343\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n344\t        shard_id: u32,\n345\t        bucket_id: usize,\n346\t        index_uid: &str,\n347\t        address: &str,\n348\t    ) -> Result<HashMap<String, u64>> {\n349\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n350\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n351\t        let mut bucket_pks = HashMap::new();\n352\t\n353\t        loop {\n354\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n355\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n356\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n357\t                filter,\n358\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n359\t                offset,\n360\t            };\n361\t\n362\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n363\t                .node_client\n364\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n365\t                .await\n366\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n367\t\n368\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n369\t                break;\n370\t            }\n371\t\n372\t            for doc in &response.results {\n373\t                let primary_key = doc\n374\t                    .get(\"id\")\n375\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n376\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n377\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n378\t\n379\t                // Check if this document belongs to the target bucket\n380\t                let doc_bucket = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n381\t                if doc_bucket == bucket_id {\n382\t                    let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n383\t                    bucket_pks.insert(primary_key.to_string(), content_hash);\n384\t                }\n385\t            }\n386\t\n387\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n388\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n389\t        }\n390\t\n391\t        Ok(bucket_pks)\n392\t    }\n393\t\n394\t    /// Compare replicas within a divergent bucket to find specific PK differences.\n395\t    ///\n396\t    /// Returns a ReplicaDiff listing:\n397\t    /// - PKs only in replica A\n398\t    /// - PKs only in replica B\n399\t    /// - PKs with different content hashes\n400\t    pub async fn compare_bucket_replicas(\n401\t        &self,\n402\t        shard_id: u32,\n403\t        bucket_id: usize,\n404\t        node_a: &NodeId,\n405\t        address_a: &str,\n406\t        node_b: &NodeId,\n407\t        address_b: &str,\n408\t        index_uid: &str,\n409\t    ) -> Result<ReplicaDiff> {\n410\t        let pks_a = self\n411\t            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_a, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_a)\n412\t            .await?;\n413\t        let pks_b = self\n414\t            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_b, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_b)\n415\t            .await?;\n416\t\n417\t        let mut a_only = Vec::new();\n418\t        let mut b_only = Vec::new();\n419\t        let mut mismatched = Vec::new();\n420\t\n421\t        // Find PKs only in A or with mismatched content\n422\t        for (pk, hash_a) in &pks_a {\n423\t            match pks_b.get(pk) {\n424\t                Some(hash_b) if hash_b != hash_a => {\n425\t                    mismatched.push(pk.clone());\n426\t                }\n427\t                None => {\n428\t                    a_only.push(pk.clone());\n429\t                }\n430\t                _ => {}\n431\t            }\n432\t        }\n433\t\n434\t        // Find PKs only in B\n435\t        for pk in pks_b.keys() {\n436\t            if !pks_a.contains_key(pk) {\n437\t                b_only.push(pk.clone());\n438\t            }\n439\t        }\n440\t\n441\t        Ok(ReplicaDiff {\n442\t            shard_id,\n443\t            a_only_pks: a_only,\n444\t            b_only_pks: b_only,\n445\t            mismatched_pks: mismatched,\n446\t        })\n447\t    }\n448\t\n449\t    /// Run a single reconciliation pass.\n450\t    pub async fn run_pass(&self) -> Result<ReconcilerPass> {\n451\t        let mut pass = ReconcilerPass {\n452\t            started_at: millis_now(),\n453\t            completed_at: 0,\n454\t            shards_scanned: 0,\n455\t            shards_with_drift: 0,\n456\t            repairs_performed: 0,\n457\t            errors: Vec::new(),\n458\t        };\n459\t\n460\t        // Set as current pass\n461\t        {\n462\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n463\t            *current = Some(pass.clone());\n464\t        }\n465\t\n466\t        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n467\t        let shard_count = topology.shards;\n468\t        let replica_groups = topology.groups().count() as u32;\n469\t\n470\t        // Determine which shards to scan\n471\t        let shards_to_scan = if self.config.shards_per_pass == 0 {\n472\t            // Scan all shards\n473\t            (0..shard_count).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n474\t        } else {\n475\t            // Scan a subset (for throttling)\n476\t            (0..shard_count)\n477\t                .take(self.config.shards_per_pass as usize)\n478\t                .collect()\n479\t        };\n480\t\n481\t        info!(\n482\t            \"Anti-entropy pass starting: {} shards to scan\",\n483\t            shards_to_scan.len()\n484\t        );\n485\t\n486\t        // Scan each shard\n487\t        for shard_id in shards_to_scan {\n488\t            match self.scan_shard(&topology, shard_id).await {\n489\t                Ok(drift_detected) => {\n490\t                    pass.shards_scanned += 1;\n491\t                    if drift_detected {\n492\t                        pass.shards_with_drift += 1;\n493\t                    }\n494\t                }\n495\t                Err(e) => {\n496\t                    pass.errors.push(format!(\"shard {}: {}\", shard_id, e));\n497\t                }\n498\t            }\n499\t        }\n500\t\n501\t        pass.completed_at = millis_now();\n502\t\n503\t        // Archive pass\n504\t        {\n505\t            let mut history = self.pass_history.write().await;\n506\t            history.push(pass.clone());\n507\t            // Keep last 100 passes\n508\t            if history.len() > 100 {\n509\t                history.remove(0);\n510\t            }\n511\t        }\n512\t\n513\t        // Clear current pass\n514\t        {\n515\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n516\t            *current = None;\n517\t        }\n518\t\n519\t        info!(\n520\t            \"Anti-entropy pass completed: {} shards scanned, {} with drift, {} repairs\",\n521\t            pass.shards_scanned, pass.shards_with_drift, pass.repairs_performed\n522\t        );\n523\t\n524\t        Ok(pass)\n525\t    }\n526\t\n527\t    /// Scan a single shard for drift.\n528\t    async fn scan_shard(&self, topology: &Topology, shard_id: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n529\t        // For each replica group, get the assigned nodes\n530\t        let mut fingerprints = Vec::new();\n531\t\n532\t        for group in topology.groups() {\n533\t            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n534\t            for node_id in assigned {\n535\t                // Look up node address from topology\n536\t                let topology_guard = self.topology.read().await;\n537\t                let node = topology_guard\n538\t                    .node(&node_id)\n539\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n540\t\n541\t                if !node.is_healthy() {\n542\t                    warn!(\"Node {} is not healthy, skipping fingerprint\", node_id);\n543\t                    continue;\n544\t                }\n545\t\n546\t                let address = node.address.clone();\n547\t                drop(topology_guard);\n548\t\n549\t                match self\n550\t                    .fingerprint_shard(&node_id, shard_id, &self.config.index_uid, &address)\n551\t                    .await\n552\t                {\n553\t                    Ok(fp) => fingerprints.push((node_id.clone(), address, fp)),\n554\t                    Err(e) => {\n555\t                        warn!(\n556\t                            \"Failed to fingerprint shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n557\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n558\t                        );\n559\t                        // Continue with other nodes\n560\t                    }\n561\t                }\n562\t            }\n563\t        }\n564\t\n565\t        if fingerprints.is_empty() {\n566\t            // No readable replicas\n567\t            return Ok(false);\n568\t        }\n569\t\n570\t        // Compare fingerprints\n571\t        let (ref_node_id, ref_address, reference) = &fingerprints[0];\n572\t        let mut drift_detected = false;\n573\t\n574\t        for (node_id, address, fp) in &fingerprints[1..] {\n575\t            if fp.merkle_root != reference.merkle_root {\n576\t                drift_detected = true;\n577\t                debug!(\n578\t                    \"Shard {} drift detected: node {} has different merkle root\",\n579\t                    shard_id, node_id\n580\t                );\n581\t\n582\t                if self.config.auto_repair {\n583\t                    // Perform detailed diff and repair\n584\t                    if let Err(e) = self\n585\t                        .repair_shard(\n586\t                            shard_id,\n587\t                            ref_node_id,\n588\t                            ref_address,\n589\t                            reference,\n590\t                            node_id,\n591\t                            address,\n592\t                            fp,\n593\t                        )\n594\t                        .await\n595\t                    {\n596\t                        error!(\n597\t                            \"Failed to repair shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n598\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n599\t                        );\n600\t                    }\n601\t                }\n602\t            }\n603\t        }\n604\t\n605\t        Ok(drift_detected)\n606\t    }\n607\t\n608\t    /// Repair a shard by comparing replicas and applying fixes (plan §13.8 step 3).\n609\t    async fn repair_shard(\n610\t        &self,\n611\t        shard_id: u32,\n612\t        reference_node: &NodeId,\n613\t        reference_address: &str,\n614\t        reference_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n615\t        target_node: &NodeId,\n616\t        target_address: &str,\n617\t        target_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n618\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n619\t        debug!(\n620\t            \"Repairing shard {} on node {} (reference: node {})\",\n621\t            shard_id, target_node, reference_node\n622\t        );\n623\t\n624\t        // Step 1: Find divergent buckets using per-bucket hashes (plan §13.8 step 2)\n625\t        let divergent_buckets = self.diff_fingerprints(reference_fp, target_fp);\n626\t\n627\t        if divergent_buckets.is_empty() {\n628\t            // No bucket-level divergence (shouldn't happen if merkle roots differ)\n629\t            warn!(\n630\t                \"Shard {} merkle roots differ but no bucket divergence found\",\n631\t                shard_id\n632\t            );\n633\t            return Ok(());\n634\t        }\n635\t\n636\t        debug!(\n637\t            \"Shard {} has {} divergent buckets out of {}\",\n638\t            shard_id,\n639\t            divergent_buckets.len(),\n640\t            BUCKET_COUNT\n641\t        );\n642\t\n643\t        // Step 2: For each divergent bucket, enumerate divergent PKs\n644\t        for bucket_id in divergent_buckets {\n645\t            match self\n646\t                .compare_bucket_replicas(\n647\t                    shard_id,\n648\t                    bucket_id,\n649\t                    reference_node,\n650\t                    reference_address,\n651\t                    target_node,\n652\t                    target_address,\n653\t                    &self.config.index_uid,\n654\t                )\n655\t                .await\n656\t            {\n657\t                Ok(diff) => {\n658\t                    let total_divergent =\n659\t                        diff.a_only_pks.len() + diff.b_only_pks.len() + diff.mismatched_pks.len();\n660\t\n661\t                    if total_divergent > 0 {\n662\t                        debug!(\n663\t                            \"Bucket {} in shard {}: {} divergent PKs ({} A-only, {} B-only, {} mismatched)\",\n664\t                            bucket_id,\n665\t                            shard_id,\n666\t                            total_divergent,\n667\t                            diff.a_only_pks.len(),\n668\t                            diff.b_only_pks.len(),\n669\t                            diff.mismatched_pks.len()\n670\t                        );\n671\t\n672\t                        // Step 3: For each divergent PK, apply repair (plan §13.8 step 3)\n673\t                        // TODO: Implement actual repair writes\n674\t                        // For now, just log the divergence\n675\t                        for pk in &diff.a_only_pks {\n676\t                            debug!(\"PK {} only exists on reference node {}\", pk, reference_node);\n677\t                        }\n678\t                        for pk in &diff.b_only_pks {\n679\t                            debug!(\"PK {} only exists on target node {}\", pk, target_node);\n680\t                        }\n681\t                        for pk in &diff.mismatched_pks {\n682\t                            debug!(\"PK {} has mismatched content\", pk);\n683\t                        }\n684\t                    }\n685\t                }\n686\t                Err(e) => {\n687\t                    warn!(\n688\t                        \"Failed to compare bucket {} in shard {}: {}\",\n689\t                        bucket_id, shard_id, e\n690\t                    );\n691\t                }\n692\t            }\n693\t        }\n694\t\n695\t        Ok(())\n696\t    }\n697\t\n698\t    /// Get pass history.\n699\t    pub async fn pass_history(&self) -> Vec<ReconcilerPass> {\n700\t        self.pass_history.read().await.clone()\n701\t    }\n702\t\n703\t    /// Get current pass in progress.\n704\t    pub async fn current_pass(&self) -> Option<ReconcilerPass> {\n705\t        self.current_pass.read().await.clone()\n706\t    }\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n710\t/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n711\t///\n712\t/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n713\t/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n714\t/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n715\t/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\n716\tpub fn validate_migration_safety(\n717\t    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n718\t    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n719\t) -> std::result::Result<(), MigrationError> {\n720\t    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n721\t        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n722\t    }\n723\t    Ok(())\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n727\t/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n728\t///\n729\t/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n730\t/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n731\t/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\n732\tpub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n733\t    if ae_enabled {\n734\t        return None;\n735\t    }\n736\t    Some(\n737\t        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n738\t         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n739\t         data loss at the cutover boundary.
