Anti-entropy reconciler module (plan §13.8).\n//!\n//! Detects and repairs replica drift using the fingerprint → diff → repair pipeline.\n//! Resolves Open Problem #1 (dual-write safety) by continuously reconciling\n//! replicas and catching any missed writes.\n//!\n//! # CDC Origin Tag (plan §13.13)\n//!\n//! Repair writes must be tagged with `origin=\"antientropy\"` so they are suppressed\n//! from CDC by default (unless `emit_internal_writes` is true).\n//!\n//! When constructing `WriteRequest` for repair writes, set:\n//! ```ignore\n//! use miroir_core::cdc::ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY;\n//! WriteRequest { ..., origin: Some(ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY.to_string()) }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::migration::{MigrationConfig, MigrationError};\nuse crate::router::assign_shard_in_group;\nuse crate::scatter::{FetchDocumentsRequest, FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeClient};\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, HashMap};\nuse std::hash::Hasher;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, warn};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Number of buckets for granular diff (plan §13.8 step 2).\n///\n/// Each bucket isolates divergence to ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of the PK space.\npub const BUCKET_COUNT: usize = 256;\n\n/// Anti-entropy configuration (plan §13.8).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AntiEntropyConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub schedule: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub shards_per_pass: u32,\n    pub max_read_concurrency: u32,\n    pub fingerprint_batch_size: u32,\n    pub auto_repair: bool,\n    pub updated_at_field: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AntiEntropyConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            schedule: \"every 6h\".to_string(),\n            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n            shards_per_pass: 0,\n            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n            auto_repair: true,\n            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Shard fingerprint for comparison.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardFingerprint {\n    /// Shard ID.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n    /// Node ID.\n    pub node_id: String,\n    /// Merkle root of document hashes.\n    pub merkle_root: String,\n    /// Document count.\n    pub document_count: u64,\n    /// Per-bucket hashes for detailed diff.\n    pub bucket_hashes: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Replica diff result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReplicaDiff {\n    /// Shard ID.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n    /// Primary keys only in replica A.\n    pub a_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Primary keys only in replica B.\n    pub b_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Primary keys with content hash mismatch.\n    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Repair action.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RepairAction {\n    /// Shard ID.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n    /// Primary key to repair.\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    /// Authoritative version (document JSON).\n    pub authoritative_doc: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Target nodes that need repair.\n    pub target_nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n    /// Reason for repair.\n    pub reason: RepairReason,\n}\n\n/// Why a repair is needed.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum RepairReason {\n    /// Document missing on replica.\n    Missing,\n    /// Content hash mismatch.\n    Mismatch,\n    /// Expired document resurrection (TTL interaction).\n    ExpiredResurrection,\n}\n\n/// Reconciler pass result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReconcilerPass {\n    /// Pass started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Pass completed at (UNIX ms).\n    pub completed_at: u64,\n    /// Shards scanned.\n    pub shards_scanned: u32,\n    /// Shards with drift detected.\n    pub shards_with_drift: u32,\n    /// Repairs performed.\n    pub repairs_performed: u32,\n    /// Errors encountered.\n    pub errors: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Anti-entropy reconciler.\npub struct AntiEntropyReconciler<C: NodeClient> {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n    /// Shared topology.\n    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n    /// Pass history.\n    pass_history: Arc<RwLock<Vec<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n    /// Currently running pass.\n    current_pass: Arc<RwLock<Option<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n    /// HTTP client for node communication.\n    node_client: Arc<C>,\n}\n\nimpl<C: NodeClient> AntiEntropyReconciler<C> {\n    /// Create a new anti-entropy reconciler.\n    pub fn new(\n        config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n        node_client: Arc<C>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            topology,\n            pass_history: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            current_pass: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n            node_client,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Compute bucket ID for a primary key (plan §13.8 step 2).\n    ///\n    /// Uses pk-hash modulo BUCKET_COUNT to isolate divergence to ~0.4% of PK space.\n    /// This is reused by §13.1 reshard verify with PK-keyed bucketing.\n    pub fn bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key: &str) -> usize {\n        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n        (hasher.finish() as usize) % BUCKET_COUNT\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the canonical content hash of a document.\n    ///\n    /// The canonical form excludes internal Miroir fields (_miroir_*, _rankingScore)\n    /// and serializes with sorted keys for deterministic hashing.\n    pub fn compute_content_hash(document: &Value) -> u64 {\n        // Remove internal fields to get canonical content\n        let mut canonical = document.clone();\n        if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object_mut() {\n            // Remove all _miroir_* fields\n            obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n            // Remove _rankingScore (not content, used for scoring)\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n\n        // Serialize with sorted keys for deterministic output\n        let canonical_json = if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object() {\n            // Use BTreeMap to sort keys\n            let sorted: BTreeMap<_, _> = obj.iter().collect();\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n        } else {\n            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n        };\n\n        // Hash using xxh3 (xxhash family, same as router)\n        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        hasher.write(canonical_json.as_bytes());\n        hasher.finish()\n    }\n\n    /// Fingerprint a single shard on a node (plan §13.8 step 1).\n    ///\n    /// Iterates all documents with filter=_miroir_shard={id}, computes\n    /// hash(primary_key || content_hash) for each, and folds into a\n    /// streaming xxh3 digest. Also computes per-bucket hashes for diff.\n    pub async fn fingerprint_shard(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        address: &str,\n    ) -> Result<ShardFingerprint> {\n        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let mut document_count = 0u64;\n        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64); // Shard-seeded digest\n\n        // Per-bucket hashers for granular diff (plan §13.8 step 2)\n        let mut bucket_hashers: Vec<XxHash64> = (0..BUCKET_COUNT)\n            .map(|_| XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64))\n            .collect();\n\n        // Paginated iteration through documents\n        loop {\n            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n                filter,\n                limit: batch_size as u32,\n                offset,\n            };\n\n            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n                .node_client\n                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n\n            if response.results.is_empty() {\n                break; // No more documents\n            }\n\n            for doc in &response.results {\n                // Extract primary key\n                let primary_key = doc\n                    .get(\"id\")\n                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n                // Compute content hash\n                let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n\n                // Fold: hash(primary_key || content_hash) into digest\n                let mut pk_hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n                pk_hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n                pk_hasher.write_u64(content_hash);\n                let doc_hash = pk_hasher.finish();\n\n                // Fold into shard digest\n                hasher.write_u64(doc_hash);\n                document_count += 1;\n\n                // Fold into bucket digest for granular diff\n                let bucket_id = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n                bucket_hashers[bucket_id].write_u64(doc_hash);\n            }\n\n            offset += batch_size as u32;\n\n            // Self-throttle: small sleep between batches to target <2% CPU\n            // (In production, this would be adaptive based on CPU metrics)\n            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n        }\n\n        let merkle_root = format!(\"xxh3:{}\", hasher.finish());\n\n        // Extract per-bucket hashes\n        let bucket_hashes: Vec<String> = bucket_hashers\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|h| format!(\"xxh3:{}\", h.finish()))\n            .collect();\n\n        debug!(\n            \"Fingerprinted shard {} on node {}: {} documents, root {}\",\n            shard_id, node_id, document_count, merkle_root\n        );\n\n        Ok(ShardFingerprint {\n            shard_id,\n            node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n            merkle_root,\n            document_count,\n            bucket_hashes,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Find divergent buckets between two fingerprints (plan §13.8 step 2).\n    ///\n    /// Returns bucket IDs where the per-bucket hashes differ.\n    /// Each bucket isolates divergence to ~0.4% of the PK space.\n    pub fn diff_fingerprints(\n        &self,\n        fp_a: &ShardFingerprint,\n        fp_b: &ShardFingerprint,\n    ) -> Vec<usize> {\n        let mut divergent = Vec::new();\n\n        if fp_a.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT || fp_b.bucket_hashes.len() != BUCKET_COUNT {\n            // Fallback: if bucket hashes aren't computed, treat all buckets as divergent\n            warn!(\n                \"Bucket hashes not computed, treating all {} buckets as divergent\",\n                BUCKET_COUNT\n            );\n            return (0..BUCKET_COUNT).collect();\n        }\n\n        for (bucket_id, (hash_a, hash_b)) in fp_a\n            .bucket_hashes\n            .iter()\n            .zip(fp_b.bucket_hashes.iter())\n            .enumerate()\n        {\n            if hash_a != hash_b {\n                divergent.push(bucket_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        divergent\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch all primary keys in a specific bucket (plan §13.8 step 2).\n    ///\n    /// Returns a map of primary key to content hash for all documents\n    /// in the given bucket on the specified replica.\n    pub async fn fetch_bucket_pks(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        bucket_id: usize,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        address: &str,\n    ) -> Result<HashMap<String, u64>> {\n        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let mut bucket_pks = HashMap::new();\n\n        loop {\n            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n                filter,\n                limit: batch_size as u32,\n                offset,\n            };\n\n            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n                .node_client\n                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n\n            if response.results.is_empty() {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            for doc in &response.results {\n                let primary_key = doc\n                    .get(\"id\")\n                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n                // Check if this document belongs to the target bucket\n                let doc_bucket = Self::bucket_for_primary_key(primary_key);\n                if doc_bucket == bucket_id {\n                    let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n                    bucket_pks.insert(primary_key.to_string(), content_hash);\n                }\n            }\n\n            offset += batch_size as u32;\n            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n        }\n\n        Ok(bucket_pks)\n    }\n\n    /// Compare replicas within a divergent bucket to find specific PK differences.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a ReplicaDiff listing:\n    /// - PKs only in replica A\n    /// - PKs only in replica B\n    /// - PKs with different content hashes\n    pub async fn compare_bucket_replicas(\n        &self,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        bucket_id: usize,\n        node_a: &NodeId,\n        address_a: &str,\n        node_b: &NodeId,\n        address_b: &str,\n        index_uid: &str,\n    ) -> Result<ReplicaDiff> {\n        let pks_a = self\n            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_a, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_a)\n            .await?;\n        let pks_b = self\n            .fetch_bucket_pks(node_b, shard_id, bucket_id, index_uid, address_b)\n            .await?;\n\n        let mut a_only = Vec::new();\n        let mut b_only = Vec::new();\n        let mut mismatched = Vec::new();\n\n        // Find PKs only in A or with mismatched content\n        for (pk, hash_a) in &pks_a {\n            match pks_b.get(pk) {\n                Some(hash_b) if hash_b != hash_a => {\n                    mismatched.push(pk.clone());\n                }\n                None => {\n                    a_only.push(pk.clone());\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Find PKs only in B\n        for pk in pks_b.keys() {\n            if !pks_a.contains_key(pk) {\n                b_only.push(pk.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(ReplicaDiff {\n            shard_id,\n            a_only_pks: a_only,\n            b_only_pks: b_only,\n            mismatched_pks: mismatched,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single reconciliation pass.\n    pub async fn run_pass(&self) -> Result<ReconcilerPass> {\n        let mut pass = ReconcilerPass {\n            started_at: millis_now(),\n            completed_at: 0,\n            shards_scanned: 0,\n            shards_with_drift: 0,\n            repairs_performed: 0,\n            errors: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Set as current pass\n        {\n            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n            *current = Some(pass.clone());\n        }\n\n        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n        let shard_count = topology.shards;\n        let replica_groups = topology.groups().count() as u32;\n\n        // Determine which shards to scan\n        let shards_to_scan = if self.config.shards_per_pass == 0 {\n            // Scan all shards\n            (0..shard_count).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        } else {\n            // Scan a subset (for throttling)\n            (0..shard_count)\n                .take(self.config.shards_per_pass as usize)\n                .collect()\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"Anti-entropy pass starting: {} shards to scan\",\n            shards_to_scan.len()\n        );\n\n        // Scan each shard\n        for shard_id in shards_to_scan {\n            match self.scan_shard(&topology, shard_id).await {\n                Ok(drift_detected) => {\n                    pass.shards_scanned += 1;\n                    if drift_detected {\n                        pass.shards_with_drift += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    pass.errors.push(format!(\"shard {}: {}\", shard_id, e));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        pass.completed_at = millis_now();\n\n        // Archive pass\n        {\n            let mut history = self.pass_history.write().await;\n            history.push(pass.clone());\n            // Keep last 100 passes\n            if history.len() > 100 {\n                history.remove(0);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Clear current pass\n        {\n            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n            *current = None;\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            \"Anti-entropy pass completed: {} shards scanned, {} with drift, {} repairs\",\n            pass.shards_scanned, pass.shards_with_drift, pass.repairs_performed\n        );\n\n        Ok(pass)\n    }\n\n    /// Scan a single shard for drift.\n    async fn scan_shard(&self, topology: &Topology, shard_id: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n        // For each replica group, get the assigned nodes\n        let mut fingerprints = Vec::new();\n\n        for group in topology.groups() {\n            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n            for node_id in assigned {\n                // Look up node address from topology\n                let topology_guard = self.topology.read().await;\n                let node = topology_guard\n                    .node(&node_id)\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n\n                if !node.is_healthy() {\n                    warn!(\"Node {} is not healthy, skipping fingerprint\", node_id);\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                let address = node.address.clone();\n                drop(topology_guard);\n\n                match self\n                    .fingerprint_shard(&node_id, shard_id, &self.config.index_uid, &address)\n                    .await\n                {\n                    Ok(fp) => fingerprints.push((node_id.clone(), address, fp)),\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        warn!(\n                            \"Failed to fingerprint shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n                            shard_id, node_id, e\n                        );\n                        // Continue with other nodes\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if fingerprints.is_empty() {\n            // No readable replicas\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        // Compare fingerprints\n        let (ref_node_id, ref_address, reference) = &fingerprints[0];\n        let mut drift_detected = false;\n\n        for (node_id, address, fp) in &fingerprints[1..] {\n            if fp.merkle_root != reference.merkle_root {\n                drift_detected = true;\n                debug!(\n                    \"Shard {} drift detected: node {} has different merkle root\",\n                    shard_id, node_id\n                );\n\n                if self.config.auto_repair {\n                    // Perform detailed diff and repair\n                    if let Err(e) = self\n                        .repair_shard(\n                            shard_id,\n                            ref_node_id,\n                            ref_address,\n                            reference,\n                            node_id,\n                            address,\n                            fp,\n                        )\n                        .await\n                    {\n                        error!(\n                            \"Failed to repair shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n                            shard_id, node_id, e\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(drift_detected)\n    }\n\n    /// Repair a shard by comparing replicas and applying fixes (plan §13.8 step 3).\n    async fn repair_shard(\n        &self,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        reference_node: &NodeId,\n        reference_address: &str,\n        reference_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n        target_node: &NodeId,\n        target_address: &str,\n        target_fp: &ShardFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        debug!(\n            \"Repairing shard {} on node {} (reference: node {})\",\n            shard_id, target_node, reference_node\n        );\n\n        // Step 1: Find divergent buckets using per-bucket hashes (plan §13.8 step 2)\n        let divergent_buckets = self.diff_fingerprints(reference_fp, target_fp);\n\n        if divergent_buckets.is_empty() {\n            // No bucket-level divergence (shouldn't happen if merkle roots differ)\n            warn!(\n                \"Shard {} merkle roots differ but no bucket divergence found\",\n                shard_id\n            );\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        debug!(\n            \"Shard {} has {} divergent buckets out of {}\",\n            shard_id,\n            divergent_buckets.len(),\n            BUCKET_COUNT\n        );\n\n        // Step 2: For each divergent bucket, enumerate divergent PKs\n        for bucket_id in divergent_buckets {\n            match self\n                .compare_bucket_replicas(\n                    shard_id,\n                    bucket_id,\n                    reference_node,\n                    reference_address,\n                    target_node,\n                    target_address,\n                    &self.config.index_uid,\n                )\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(diff) => {\n                    let total_divergent =\n                        diff.a_only_pks.len() + diff.b_only_pks.len() + diff.mismatched_pks.len();\n\n                    if total_divergent > 0 {\n                        debug!(\n                            \"Bucket {} in shard {}: {} divergent PKs ({} A-only, {} B-only, {} mismatched)\",\n                            bucket_id,\n                            shard_id,\n                            total_divergent,\n                            diff.a_only_pks.len(),\n                            diff.b_only_pks.len(),\n                            diff.mismatched_pks.len()\n                        );\n\n                        // Step 3: For each divergent PK, apply repair (plan §13.8 step 3)\n                        // TODO: Implement actual repair writes\n                        // For now, just log the divergence\n                        for pk in &diff.a_only_pks {\n                            debug!(\"PK {} only exists on reference node {}\", pk, reference_node);\n                        }\n                        for pk in &diff.b_only_pks {\n                            debug!(\"PK {} only exists on target node {}\", pk, target_node);\n                        }\n                        for pk in &diff.mismatched_pks {\n                            debug!(\"PK {} has mismatched content\", pk);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\n                        \"Failed to compare bucket {} in shard {}: {}\",\n                        bucket_id, shard_id, e\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get pass history.\n    pub async fn pass_history(&self) -> Vec<ReconcilerPass> {\n        self.pass_history.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pass in progress.\n    pub async fn current_pass(&self) -> Option<ReconcilerPass> {\n        self.current_pass.read().await.clone()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n///\n/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\npub fn validate_migration_safety(\n    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n) -> std::result::Result<(), MigrationError> {\n    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n///\n/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\npub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n    if ae_enabled {\n        return None;\n    }\n    Some(\n        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n         data loss at the cutover boundary. Re-enable anti-entropy for defense-in-depth.\"\n            .to_string(),\n    )\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_safe_with_delta_pass() {\n        let ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n            enabled: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let mc = MigrationConfig {\n            skip_delta_pass: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn        skip_delta_pass: true,\n            anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_err());\n    }\n\n MigrationConfig {\n            skip_delta_pass: true,\n            anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_warning_when_ae_disabled() {\n        assert!(migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(false).is_some());\n           \"id\": \"test-1\",\n            \"title\": \"Test Document\",\n            \"content\": \"Some content\",\n        });\n\n        let hash2 = AntiEntropyReconciler::<crate::scatter::MockNodeClient>::compute_content_hash(&doc_clean);\n\n        // Same content (without internal fields) should produce same hash\n        assert_eq!(\n Serialize, Deserialize)]\n112\tpub struct WriteRequest {\n113\t    pub index_uid: String,\n114\t    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n115\t    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n116\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n117\t    /// - None = client write (always emitted to CDC)\n118\t    /// - Some(\"antientropy\") = anti-entropy repair write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n119\t    /// - Some(\"reshard_backfill\") = reshard backfill write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n120\t    /// - Some(\"rollover\") = ILM rollover write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n121\t    /// - Some(\"ttl_expire\") = TTL expiration delete (suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes)\n122\t    ///\n123\t    /// This field is NEVER stored on documents and never returned to clients.\n124\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n125\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\t/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n129\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n130\tpub struct WriteResponse {\n131\t    pub success: bool,\n132\t    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n133\t    pub message: Option<String>,\n134\t    pub code: Option<String>,\n135\t    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Request to delete documents by IDs.\n139\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n140\tpub struct DeleteByIdsRequest {\n141\t    pub index_uid: String,\n142\t    pub ids: Vec<String>,\n143\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n144\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n145\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n149\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n150\tpub struct DeleteByFilterRequest {\n151\t    pub index_uid: String,\n152\t    pub filter: Value,\n153\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n154\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n155\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t/// Response from a delete operation.\n159\tpub type DeleteResponse = WriteResponse;\n160\t\n161\t/// Request to fetch documents with a filter (used for shard migration).\n162\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n163\tpub struct FetchDocumentsRequest {\n164\t    pub index_uid: String,\n165\t    pub filter: Value,\n166\t    pub limit: u32,\n167\t    pub offset: u32,\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\t/// Response from a fetch documents operation.\n171\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n172\tpub struct FetchDocumentsResponse {\n173\t    pub results: Vec<Value>,\n174\t    pub limit: u32,\n175\t    pub offset: u32,\n176\t    pub total: u64,\n177\t}\n178\t\n179\t/// Request to get task status from a node.\n180\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n181\tpub struct TaskStatusRequest {\n182\t    /// The task UID to query\n183\t    pub task_uid: u64,\n184\t}\n185\t\n186\t/// Response from a single node's task status query.\n187\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n188\tpub struct TaskStatusResponse {\n189\t    /// The task UID\n190\t    pub task_uid: u64,\n191\t    /// Current task status\n192\t    pub status: String,\n193\t    /// Error message if failed\n194\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n195\t    /// Error type if failed\n196\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n197\t    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\timpl TaskStatusResponse {\n201\t    /// Convert Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n202\t    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n203\t        match self.status.as_str() {\n204\t            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n205\t            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n206\t            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n207\t            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n208\t            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n209\t        }\n210\t    }\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n214\t// NodeClient trait\n215\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n216\t\n217\t/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n218\t#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\n219\tpub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n220\t    async fn search_node(\n221\t        &self,\n222\t        node: &NodeId,\n223\t        address: &str,\n224\t        request: &SearchRequest,\n225\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n226\t\n227\t    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n228\t    async fn preflight_node(\n229\t        &self,\n230\t        _node: &NodeId,\n231\t        _address: &str,\n232\t        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n233\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n234\t        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n235\t    }\n236\t\n237\t    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n238\t    async fn write_documents(\n239\t        &self,\n240\t        _node: &NodeId,\n241\t        _address: &str,\n242\t        _request: &WriteRequest,\n243\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n244\t        Ok(WriteResponse {\n245\t            success: false,\n246\t            task_uid: None,\n247\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n248\t            code: None,\n249\t            error_type: None,\n250\t        })\n251\t    }\n252\t\n253\t    /// Get task status from a node.\n254\t    fn get_task_status(\n255\t        &self,\n256\t        _node: &NodeId,\n257\t        _address: &str,\n258\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n259\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n260\t        async move {\n261\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n262\t                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n263\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n264\t                error: None,\n265\t                error_type: None,\n266\t            })\n267\t        }\n268\t    }\n269\t\n270\t    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n271\t    async fn delete_documents(\n272\t        &self,\n273\t        _node: &NodeId,\n274\t        _address: &str,\n275\t        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n276\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n277\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n278\t            success: false,\n279\t            task_uid: None,\n280\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n281\t            code: None,\n282\t            error_type: None,\n283\t        })\n284\t    }\n285\t\n286\t    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n287\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n288\t        &self,\n289\t        _node: &NodeId,\n290\t        _address: &str,\n291\t        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n292\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n293\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n294\t            success: false,\n295\t            task_uid: None,\n296\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n297\t            code: None,\n298\t            error_type: None,\n299\t        })\n300\t    }\n301\t\n302\t    /// Fetch documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n303\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n304\t        &self,\n305\t        _node: &NodeId,\n306\t        _address: &str,\n307\t        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n308\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n309\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n310\t            results: Vec::new(),\n311\t            limit: _request.limit,\n312\t            offset: _request.offset,\n313\t            total: 0,\n314\t        })\n315\t    }\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n319\tpub enum NodeError {\n320\t    Timeout,\n321\t    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n322\t    NetworkError(String),\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n326\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n327\t    pub index_uid: String,\n328\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n329\t    pub offset: usize,\n330\t    pub limit: usize,\n331\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n332\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n333\t    pub ranking_score: bool,\n334\t    pub body: Value,\n335\t    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n336\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\timpl SearchRequest {\n340\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n341\t    ///\n342\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n343\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n344\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n345\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n346\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n347\t\n348\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n349\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n350\t\n351\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n352\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n353\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n354\t\n355\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n356\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n357\t\n358\t        // Ensure query is set\n359\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n360\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n364\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n365\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n366\t        }\n367\t\n368\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n369\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n370\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n371\t        }\n372\t\n373\t        body\n374\t    }\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t#[derive(Debug)]\n378\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n379\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n380\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n381\t    pub partial: bool,\n382\t    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n383\t}\n384\t\n385\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n386\tpub fn plan_search_scatter(\n387\t    topology: &Topology,\n388\t    query_seq: u64,\n389\t    rf: usize,\n390\t    shard_count: u32,\n391\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n392\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n393\t\n394\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n395\t        Some(g) => g,\n396\t        None => {\n397\t            return ScatterPlan {\n398\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n399\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n400\t            };\n401\t        }\n402\t    };\n403\t\n404\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n405\t\n406\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n407\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n408\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n409\t        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n410\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n411\t    }\n412\t\n413\t    ScatterPlan {\n414\t        chosen_group,\n415\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n416\t        shard_to_node,\n417\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n418\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n419\t    }\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n423\t///\n424\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n425\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n426\tpub fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n427\t    topology: &Topology,\n428\t    query_seq: u64,\n429\t    rf: usize,\n430\t    shard_count: u32,\n431\t    index: &str,\n432\t    floor: u64,\n433\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n434\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n435\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n436\t\n437\t    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n438\t\n439\t    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n440\t        shard_count,\n441\t        group,\n442\t        rf,\n443\t        query_seq,\n444\t        index,\n445\t        floor,\n446\t        version_checker,\n447\t    )?;\n448\t\n449\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n450\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n451\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n452\t        // Filter by version floor, then rotate by query_seq\n453\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n454\t            .iter()\n455\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n456\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n457\t                version >= floor\n458\t            })\n459\t            .collect();\n460\t\n461\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n462\t            return None;\n463\t        }\n464\t\n465\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n466\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n467\t    }\n468\t\n469\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n470\t        chosen_group,\n471\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n472\t        shard_to_node,\n473\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n474\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n475\t    })\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n479\t///\n480\t/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n481\t/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\n482\tpub fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n483\t    topology: &Topology,\n484\t    query_seq: u64,\n485\t    rf: usize,\n486\t    shard_count: u32,\n487\t    pinned_group: u32,\n488\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n489\t    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n490\t\n491\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n492\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n493\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n494\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n495\t            continue;\n496\t        }\n497\t        let selected = replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone();\n498\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n502\t        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n503\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n504\t        shard_to_node,\n505\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n506\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n507\t    })\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n511\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n512\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n513\t    client: &C,\n514\t    req: SearchRequest,\n515\t    topology: &Topology,\n516\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n517\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n518\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n519\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n520\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n521\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n522\t        }\n523\t    }\n524\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n525\t\n526\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n527\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n528\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n529\t\n530\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n531\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n532\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n533\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n534\t            None => {\n535\t                for shard_id in shards {\n536\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n537\t                }\n538\t                continue;\n539\t            }\n540\t        };\n541\t        let client_ref = client;\n542\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n543\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n544\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n545\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n546\t        let span = info_span!(\n547\t            \"scatter_node\",\n548\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n549\t            address = %node.address,\n550\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n551\t        );\n552\t        tasks.push(async move {\n553\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n554\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n555\t        }.instrument(span));\n556\t    }\n557\t\n558\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n559\t\n560\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n561\t        match result {\n562\t            Ok(body) => {\n563\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n564\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n565\t                }\n566\t            }\n567\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n568\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n569\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n570\t            }\n571\t            Err(e) => {\n572\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n573\t            }\n574\t        }\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n578\t\n579\t    match policy {\n580\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n581\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n582\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n583\t            }\n584\t        }\n585\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n586\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n587\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n588\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n589\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n590\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n591\t\n592\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n593\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n594\t\n595\t                    // Try each other replica group\n596\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n597\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n598\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n599\t                        }\n600\t\n601\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n602\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n603\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n604\t                                continue;\n605\t                            }\n606\t\n607\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n608\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n609\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n610\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n611\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n612\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n613\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n614\t                                            break;\n615\t                                        }\n616\t                                        Err(_) => {\n617\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n618\t                                        }\n619\t                                    }\n620\t                                }\n621\t                            }\n622\t\n623\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n624\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n625\t                            }\n626\t                        }\n627\t                    }\n628\t\n629\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n630\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n631\t                    }\n632\t                }\n633\t\n634\t                // Merge fallback results\n635\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n636\t\n637\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n638\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n639\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n640\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n641\t                        shard_pages,\n642\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n643\t                        partial: false,\n644\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n645\t                    });\n646\t                } else {\n647\t                    // Some shards still failed\n648\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n649\t                }\n650\t            }\n651\t        }\n652\t    }\n653\t\n654\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n655\t}\n656\t\n657\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n658\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n659\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n660\t    client: &C,\n661\t    req: SearchRequest,\n662\t    topology: &Topology,\n663\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n664\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n665\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n666\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n667\t\n668\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n669\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n670\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n671\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n672\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n673\t            });\n674\t        }\n675\t    }\n676\t\n677\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n678\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n679\t\n680\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n681\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n682\t        offset: req.offset,\n683\t        limit: req.limit,\n684\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n685\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n686\t        failed_shards,\n687\t    };\n688\t\n689\t    // Span for the merge operation\n690\t    let _span = info_span!(\n691\t        \"merge\",\n692\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n693\t        offset = req.offset,\n694\t        limit = req.limit,\n695\t    ).entered();\n696\t\n697\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n701\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n702\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n703\t\n704\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n705\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n706\t    match query {\n707\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n708\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n709\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n710\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n711\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n712\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n713\t            }\n714\t            terms\n715\t        }\n716\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n717\t    }\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n721\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n722\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n723\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n724\t    client: &C,\n725\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n726\t    topology: &Topology,\n727\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n728\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n729\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n730\t    }\n731\t\n732\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n733\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n734\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n735\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n736\t        }\n737\t    }\n738\t\n739\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n740\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n741\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n742\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n743\t            None => continue,\n744\t        };\n745\t        let client_ref = client;\n746\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n747\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n748\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n749\t    }\n750\t\n751\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n752\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n753\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n754\t}\n755\t\n756\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n757\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n758\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n759\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n760\t    client: &C,\n761\t    req: SearchRequest,\n762\t    topology: &Topology,\n763\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n764\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n765\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n766\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n767\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n768\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n769\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n770\t    };\n771\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n772\t    let mut search_req = req;\n773\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n774\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n775\t}\n776\t\n777\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n778\t// Mock client\n779\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n780\t\n781\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n782\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n783\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n784\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n785\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n786\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n787\t}\n788\t\n789\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n790\t    async fn search_node(\n791\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n792\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n793\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n794\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n795\t        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n796\t            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n797\t        }))\n798\t    }\n799\t\n800\t    async fn preflight_node(\n801\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n802\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n803\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n804\t        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n805\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n806\t        }))\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    async fn write_documents(\n810\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n811\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n812\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n813\t        Ok(WriteResponse {\n814\t            success: true,\n815\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n816\t            message: None,\n817\t            code: None,\n818\t            error_type: None,\n819\t        })\n820\t    }\n821\t\n822\t    async fn delete_documents(\n823\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n824\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n825\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n826\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n827\t            success: true,\n828\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n829\t            message: None,\n830\t            code: None,\n831\t            error_type: None,\n832\t        })\n833\t    }\n834\t\n835\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n836\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n837\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n838\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n839\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n840\t            success: true,\n841\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n842\t            message: None,\n843\t            code: None,\n844\t            error_type: None,\n845\t        })\n846\t    }\n847\t\n848\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n849\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n850\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n851\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n852\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n853\t            results: Vec::new(),\n854\t            limit: request.limit,\n855\t            offset: request.offset,\n856\t            total: 0,\n857\t        })\n858\t    }\n859\t\n860\t    fn get_task_status(\n861\t        &self,\n862\t        node: &NodeId,\n863\t        _address: &str,\n864\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n865\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n866\t        let node = node.clone();\n867\t        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n868\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n869\t        async move {\n870\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n871\t                return Err(err);\n872\t            }\n873\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n874\t                task_uid,\n875\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n876\t                error: None,\n877\t                error_type: None,\n878\t            })\n879\t        }\n880\t    }\n881\t}\n882\t\n883\t#[cfg(test)]\n884\tmod tests {\n885\t    use super::*;\n886\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n887\t\n888\t    fn make_test_topology() assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n1009\t    }\n1010\t\n1011\t    #[test]\n1012\t    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1013\t        let resp = vec![\n1014\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n1015\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n1019\t        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n1020\t        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n1021\t    }\n1022\t\n1023\t assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n1028\t    }\n1029\t\n1030\t    #[tokio::test]\n1031\t    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n1032\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1033\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1034\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1035\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1036\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1037\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n1038\t        });\n1039\t = make_test_topology();\n1056\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1057\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1058\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1059\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1060\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1061\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n1062\t        });\n1063\t        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1064\t        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n1065\t        assert!(!r.degraded);\n1066\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n1067\t    }\n1068\t\n1069\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1070\t    ///\n1071\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1072\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1073\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1074\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1075\t    ///\n1076\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1077\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1078\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1079\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1080\t    ///\n1081\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1082\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1083\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1084\t    ///\n1085\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1086\t    #[tokio::test]\n1087\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1088\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1089\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1090\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1091\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1092\t\n1093\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n1094\t\n1095\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1096\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1097\t\n1098\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1099\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1100\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1101\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1102\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1103\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1104\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1105\t            ]),\n1106\t        });\n1107\t\n1108\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1109\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1110\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1111\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1112\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1113\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1114\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1115\t            ]),\n1116\t        });\n1117\t\n1118\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1119\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1120\t            total_docs: 10,\n1121\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1122\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1123\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1124\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1125\t            ]),\n1126\t        });\n1127\t\n1128\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1129\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1130\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1131\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1132\t            filter: None,\n1133\t        };\n1134\t\n1135\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1136\t\n1137\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1138\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1139\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1140\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1141\t\n1142\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1143\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1144\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1145\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1146\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1147\t\n1148\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1149\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1150\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1151\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1152\t\n1153\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1154\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1155\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1156\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1157\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1158\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1159\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1160\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1161\t    }\n1162\t\n1163\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1164\t
