      })\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let node = node.clone();\n        let address = address.to_string();\n        let request = request.clone();\n        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/').to_string();\n        let master_key = self.master_key.clone();\n        let client = self.client.clone();\n\n        async move {\n            let start = Instant::now();\n\n            tracing::debug!(\n                target: \"miroir.node\",\n                node_id = %node,\n                address = %address,\n                index = %request.index_uid,\n                operation = \"preflight\",\n                term_count = request.terms.len(),\n                \"node call started\"\n            );\n\n            // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n            let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n            let stats_resp = client\n                .get(&stats_url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n                // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n                return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                    total_docs: 0,\n                    avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n                    term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n                });\n            }\n\n            let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n                .json()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let total_docs = stats_body\n                .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                .unwrap_or(0);\n\n            // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n            let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n            let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n            for term in &request.terms {\n                let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n\n                let search_resp = client\n                    .post(&search_url)\n                    .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                    .json(&search_body)\n                    .send()\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n                if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n                    let body: Value = search_resp\n                        .json()\n                        .await\n                        .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n                    let df = body\n                        .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                        .unwrap_or(0);\n                    term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n                }\n            }\n\n            // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n            //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n            let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n\n            let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n            tracing::debug!(\n                target: \"miroir.node\",\n                node_id = %node,\n                address = %address,\n                operation = \"preflight\",\n                duration_ms,\n                total_docs,\n                \"node call completed\"\n            );\n\n            Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                total_docs,\n                avg_doc_length,\n                term_stats,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let task_uid = request.task_uid;\n        let url = Self::task_url_static(address, task_uid);\n        let master_key = self.master_key.clone();\n        let client = self.client.clone();\n\n        async move {\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response\n                .text()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                    status: status.as_u16(),\n                    body: body_text,\n                });\n            }\n\n            // Parse successful response\n            let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: json.get(\"status\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"enqueued\")\n                    .to_string(),\n                error: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"message\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                error_type: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"type\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fetch_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let address = address.to_string();\n        let request = request.clone();\n        let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            request.index_uid\n        );\n        let master_key = self.master_key.clone();\n        let client = self.client.clone();\n\n        async move {\n            let mut query_params = Vec::new();\n            query_params.push((\"limit\", request.limit.to_string()));\n            query_params.push((\"offset\", request.offset.to_string()));\n\n            if let Some(filter_obj) = request.filter.as_object() {\n                if !filter_obj.is_empty() {\n                    // Convert filter to Meilisearch filter syntax\n                    let filter_str = serde_json::to_string(&request.filter)\n                        .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to serialize filter: {}\", e)))?;\n                    query_params.push((\"filter\", filter_str));\n                }\n            }\n\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .query(&query_params)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response\n                .text()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                    status: status.as_u16(),\n                    body: body_text,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            let results = json.get(\"results\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            state,\n            headers,\n            sid,\n            client_requested_score,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n            ) {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n            );\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    state: Arc<AppState>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // Extract session ID if provided\n    let sid = session_id;\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n    // should have identical shard layout)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n            primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n            ) {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n            );\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, primary_target\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n        query: body.q.clone(),\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add settings headers\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n        primary_target = %primary_target,\n        target_count = targets.len(),\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"multi-target search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\npub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        if !client_requested_score {\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private data\".to_string()),\n            offset: Some(0),\n            limit: Some(20),\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!({\"email\": \"user@example.com\"})),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".to_string()]),\n            ranking_score: Some(false),\n            rest: serde_json::json!({}),\n        };\n        let debug_output = format!(\"{:?}\", body);\n\n        assert!(\n            !debug_output.contains(\"sensitive\"),\n            \"Debug output should not contain raw query text\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            !debug_output.contains(\"user@example.com\"),\n            \"Debug output should not contain filter values\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            debug_output.contains(\"[redacted]\"),\n            \"Debug output should show [redacted] for sensitive fields\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":38,"newStart":43,"newLines":53,"lines":["     }"," }"," ","-#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]"," impl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {","-    async fn &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError>> + Send {\n Multi-search batch API endpoint (plan §13.11).\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRef, State},\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Json,\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    merger::{ScoreMergeStrategy, MergeStrategy},\n    multi_search::{MultiSearchExecutor, SearchResultData, MultiSearchResponse},\n    scatter::{dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, SearchRequest, NodeClient},\n    topology::Topology,\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Instant;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Multi-search state.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct MultiSearchState {\n    pub config: Arc<miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig>,\n    pub topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n    pub metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    pub alias_registry: Arc<miroir_core::alias::AliasRegistry>,\n}\n\n/// Multi-search request (plan §13.11).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]\npub struct MultiSearchRequest {\n    pub queries: Vec<SingleSearchQuery>,\n}\n\n/// A single query in the batch.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]\npub struct SingleSearchQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub q: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub filter: Option<serde_json::Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub sort: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub limit: Option<usize>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub offset: Option<usize>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub rest: Value,\n}\n\n/// Search response (matches Meilisearch response format).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Default)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<serde_json::Value>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub offset: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n    pub query: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub facet_distribution: Option<std::collections::BTreeMap<String, std::collections::BTreeMap<String, u64>>>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub degraded: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub failed_shards: Option<Vec<u32>>,\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: Arc<crate::middleware::Metrics>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>, metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics: Arc::new(metrics) }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\n/// POST /multi-search — execute multiple searches in a single batch.\n///\n/// Plan §13.11: Reduces round-trips for search UIs that need results + facets\n/// from multiple queries per page render. Each query runs in parallel.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_count = body.queries.len()))]\npub async fn multi_search<S>(\n    State(state): State<MultiSearchState>,\n    Json(body): Json<MultiSearchRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<MultiSearchResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    MultiSearchState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    if !state.config.multi_search.enabled {\n        return Err(StatusCode::NOT_IMPLEMENTED);\n    }\n\n    if body.queries.is_empty() {\n        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n    }\n\n    if body.queries.len() > state.config.multi_search.max_queries_per_batch as usize {\n        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n    }\n\n    let executor = MultiSearchExecutor::new(state.config.multi_search.clone());\n\n    // Get topology and policy once for all queries\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        state.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let node_client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Convert MultiSearchRequest to core MultiSearchRequest\n    // Resolve aliases for each query (plan §13.7)\n    let mut queries_with_resolutions = Vec::new();\n    for mut q in body.queries {\n        // Resolve alias to concrete index UID(s)\n        let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n            let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&q.index_uid).await;\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&q.index_uid);\n            if targets != vec![q.index_uid.clone()] {\n                // It's an alias\n                (q.index_uid.clone(), targets)\n            } else {\n                // Not an alias\n                (q.index_uid.clone(), vec![q.index_uid.clone()])\n            }\n        } else {\n            (q.index_uid.clone(), vec![q.index_uid.clone()])\n        };\n\n        // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for the query\n        // (multi-target alias fanout is handled by expanding queries in future)\n        q.index_uid = effective_index;\n\n        let filter_str = q.filter.as_ref()\n            .and_then(|v| if v.is_null() || v.is_string() && v.as_str().map(|s| s.is_empty()).unwrap_or(false) {\n                None\n            } else {\n                serde_json::to_string(v).ok()\n            });\n\n        queries_with_resolutions.push((q, filter_str, resolved_targets));\n    }\n\n    let core_request = miroir_core::multi_search::MultiSearchRequest {\n        queries: queries_with_resolutions.into_iter().map(|(q, filter_str, _resolved_targets)| {\n            miroir_core::multi_search::SearchQuery {\n                indexUid: q.index_uid,\n                q: q.q,\n                filter: filter_str,\n                limit: q.limit,\n                offset: q.offset,\n                other: {\n                    let mut map = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n                    if let Some(sort) = q.sort {\n                        map.insert(\"sort\".to_string(), serde_json::to_value(sort).unwrap());\n                    }\n                    if let Some(facets) = q.facets {\n                        map.insert(\"facets\".to_string(), serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap());\n                    }\n                    if let Some(ranking_score) = q.ranking_score {\n                        map.insert(\"rankingScore\".to_string(), serde_json::to_value(ranking_score).unwrap());\n                    }\n                    // Add any additional fields from rest\n                    if let Ok(obj) = serde_json::from_value::<std::collections::HashMap<String, Value>>(q.rest) {\n                        for (k, v) in obj {\n                            map.entry(k).or_insert(v);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    map\n                },\n            }\n        }).collect()\n    };\n\n    // Execute multi-search with scatter-gather\n    let response = executor\n        .execute(core_request, move |query| {\n            let topology = topology.clone();\n            let node_client = node_client.clone();\n            let config = state.config.clone();\n            let strategy = strategy.clone();\n            let policy = policy;\n\n            async move {\n                let start = Instant::now();\n\n                // Plan scatter for this query\n                let plan = plan_search_scatter(\n                    &topology,\n                    0,\n                    config.replication_factor as usize,\n                    config.shards,\n                );\n\n                // Build search request\n                let filter_value = query.filter.as_ref()\n                    .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str::<Value>(s).ok());\n                let search_req = SearchRequest {\n                    index_uid: query.indexUid.clone(),\n                    query: query.q.clone(),\n                    offset: query.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n                    limit: query.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n                    filter: filter_value,\n                    facets: query.other.get(\"facets\").and_then(|v| {\n                        serde_json::from_value::<Vec<String>>(v.clone()).ok()\n                    }),\n                    ranking_score: query.other.get(\"rankingScore\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n                        .unwrap_or(false),\n                    body: serde_json::json!(query.other),\n                    global_idf: None,\n                };\n\n                // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n                match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n                    plan,\n                    &node_client,\n                    search_req,\n                    &topology,\n                    policy,\n                    &strategy as &dyn MergeStrategy,\n                )\n                .await\n                {\n                    Ok(result) => {\n                        // Strip internal fields from hits\n                        let mut hits = result.hits;\n                        for hit in &mut hits {\n                            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                                obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n                            }\n                        }\n\n                        let response_limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n                        let response_offset = query.offset.unwrap_or(0);\n\n                        let body = serde_json::json!({\n                            \"hits\": hits,\n                            \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n                            \"limit\": response_limit,\n                            \"offset\": response_offset,\n                            \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n                            \"query\": query.q,\n                        });\n\n                        let mut search_response = SearchResponse {\n                            hits,\n                            estimated_total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n                            limit: response_limit,\n                            offset: response_offset,\n                            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n                            query: query.q,\n                            facet_distribution: result.facet_distribution,\n                            degraded: if result.degraded { Some(true) } else { None },\n                            failed_shards: if !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                                Some(result.failed_shards)\n                            } else {\n                                None\n                            },\n                        };\n\n                        debug!(\n                            index = %query.indexUid,\n                            duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis(),\n                            hits = search_response.hits.len(),\n                            \"multi-search query completed\"\n                        );\n\n                        Ok(SearchResultData { body: serde_json::to_value(search_response).unwrap() })\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        debug!(\n                            index = %query.indexUid,\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"multi-search query failed\"\n                        );\n                        Err(e)\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        })\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-search execution failed\");\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n        })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(response))\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    MultiSearchState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    axum::Router::new().route(\"/\", test_diff_fingerprints_isolates_divergence() {\n475\t    // Test that divergent buckets isolate to ~0.4% of PK space\n476\t    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n477\t    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockTestNodeClient>::new(\n478\t        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n479\t        topology,\n480\t        Arc::new(MockTestNodeClient::new()),\n481\t    );\n482\t\n483\t    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n484\t    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n485\t        shard_id: 0,\n486\t        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n487\t        merkle_root: \"xxh3:123\".to_string(),\n488\t        document_count: 10000,\n489\t        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n490\t    };\n491\t\n492\t 10000,\n497\t        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n498\t    };\n499\t\n500\t    // Make 100 buckets divergent\n501\t    for i in 0..100 {\n502\t        fp_b.bucket_hashes[i] = format!(\"xxh3:divergent-{}\", i);\n503\t    }\n504\t\n505\t    let divergent = reconciler.diff_fingerprints(&fp_a, &fp_b);\n506\t\n507\t    assert_eq!(divergent.len(), 100);\n508\t\n509\t    // Each divergent bucket represents ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of PK space\n510\t    // 100 buckets ≈ 39% of total PK space\n511\t    let isolation_ratio = divergent.len() as f64 / BUCKET_COUNT as f64;\n512\t    assert!((isolation_ratio - (100.0 / 256.0)).abs() < 0.01);\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t#[tokio::test]\n516\tasync fn test_bucket_count_constant() {\n517\t    // Verify MockTestNodeClient::new();\n    mock_client.expect_fetch_documents().returning({\n        let mut call_count = 0;\n        move |_, _, req| {\n            let docs = if call_count < 2 { offset: req.offset,\n                total: 1,\n            })\n        }\n    });\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = vec![doc_a.clone()] } else { vec![] };\n            call_count += 1;\n\n            Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n                results: docs,\n                limit: req.limit,\n                offset: req.offset,\n                total: if has_doc { 1 } else { 0 },\n            })\n        }\n    });\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string());\n\n    let diff = reconciler\n        .compare_bucket_replicas(\n            0,\n            bucket_id,\n            &node_a,\n            \"http://localhost\",\n test_compare_bucket_replicas_mismatched_content() {\n    // Test same PK but different content (different content hash)\n    let doc_a = json!({ \"id\": \"key-mismatch\", \"title\": \"Version A\", AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockTestNodeClient>::bucket_for_primary_key(\"key-mismatch\");\n\n    let mut mock_client = MockTestNodeClient::new();\n    mock_client.expect_fetch_documents().returning({\n   _ => vec![],\n            };\n            call_count += 1;\n\n            Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n                results: docs,\n                limit: req.limit,\n                offset: req.offset,\n                total: 1,\n            })\n        }\n    });\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::new(\n        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n        topology,\n        Arc::new(mock_client),\n    );\n\n    let node_a = NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string());\n    let node_b = NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string());\n\n    let diff = reconciler\n        .compare_bucket_replicas(\n            0,\n            bucket_id,\n            &node_a,\n            \"http://localhost\",\n test_diff_fingerprints_isolates_divergence() {\n    // Test that divergent buckets isolate to ~0.4% of PK space\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockTestNodeClient>::new(\n        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n        topology,\n        Arc::new(MockTestNodeClient::new()),\n    );\n\n    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:123\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    let mut fp_b = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-b\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:456\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    // Make 100 buckets divergent\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        fp_b.bucket_hashes[i] = format!(\"xxh3:divergent-{}\", i);\n    }\n\n    let divergent = reconciler.diff_fingerprints(&fp_a, &fp_b);\n\n    assert_eq!(divergent.len(), 100);\n\n    // Each divergent bucket represents ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of PK space\n    // 100 buckets ≈ 39% of total PK space\n    let isolation_ratio = divergent.len() as f64 / BUCKET_COUNT as f64;\n    assert!((isolation_ratio - (100.0 / 256.0)).abs() < 0.01);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_bucket_count_constant() {\n    // Verify BUCKET_COUNT is 256 as specified in the plan\n    assert_eq!(BUCKET_COUNT, 256);\n}\n","numLines":520,"startLine":1,"totalLines":520}}}

test_diff_fingerprints_isolates_divergence() {\n    // Test that divergent buckets isolate to ~0.4% of PK space\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::<TestNodeClient>::new(\n        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n        topology,\n        Arc::new(TestNodeClient::new()),\n    );\n\n    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:123\".to_string(),\n        document_count: \"node-b\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:456\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    // Make 100 buckets divergent\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        fp_b.bucket_hashes[i] = format!(\"xxh3:divergent-{}\", i);\n    }\n\n    let divergent = reconciler.diff_fingerprints(&fp_a, &fp_b);\n\n    assert_eq!(divergent.len(), 100);\n\n    // Each divergent bucket represents ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of PK space\n    // 100 buckets ≈ 39% of total PK space\n    let isolation_ratio = divergent.len() as f64 / BUCKET_COUNT as f64;\n    assert!((isolation_ratio - (100.0 / 256.0)).abs() < 0.01);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_bucket_count_constant() {\n    // Verify BUCKET_COUNT test_diff_fingerprints_isolates_divergence() {\n    // Test that divergent buckets isolate to ~0.4% of PK space\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::<TestNodeClient>::new(\n        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n        topology,\n        Arc::new(TestNodeClient::new()),\n    );\n\n    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:123\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    let mut fp_b = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-b\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:456\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    // Make 100 buckets divergent\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        fp_b.bucket_hashes[i] = format!(\"xxh3:divergent-{}\", i);\n    }\n\n    let divergent = reconciler.diff_fingerprints(&fp_a, &fp_b);\n\n    assert_eq!(divergent.len(), 100);\n\n    // Each divergent bucket represents ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of PK space\n    // 100 buckets ≈ 39% of total PK space\n    let isolation_ratio = divergent.len() as f64 / BUCKET_COUNT as f64;\n    assert!((isolation_ratio - (100.0 / 256.0)).abs() < 0.01);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_bucket_count_constant() {\n    // Verify BUCKET_COUNT AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockTestNodeClient>::bucket_for_primary_key(pk);\n\n    assert_eq!(bucket1, bucket2, \"bucket assignment should be deterministic\");\n    assert!(bucket1 < BUCKET_COUNT,       buckets.insert(bucket);\n    }\n\n    // With 1000 keys and 256 buckets, we should hit many buckets\n    // (statistically, almost all of them)\n    assert!(buckets.len() > 200, \"keys should test_diff_fingerprints_isolates_divergence() {\n    // Test that divergent buckets isolate to ~0.4% of PK space\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)));\n    let reconciler = AntiEntropyReconciler::<MockTestNodeClient>::new(\n        AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n        topology,\n        Arc::new(MockTestNodeClient::new()),\n    );\n\n    // Create a fingerprint with 100 divergent buckets\n    let mut fp_a = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-a\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:123\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    let mut fp_b = ShardFingerprint {\n        shard_id: 0,\n        node_id: \"node-b\".to_string(),\n        merkle_root: \"xxh3:456\".to_string(),\n        document_count: 10000,\n        bucket_hashes: (0..BUCKET_COUNT).map(|_| \"xxh3:same\".to_string()).collect(),\n    };\n\n    // Make 100 buckets divergent\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        fp_b.bucket_hashes[i] = format!(\"xxh3:divergent-{}\", i);\n    }\n\n    let divergent = reconciler.diff_fingerprints(&fp_a, &fp_b);\n\n    assert_eq!(divergent.len(), 100);\n\n    // Each divergent bucket represents ~1/256 (≈0.4%) of PK space\n    // 100 buckets ≈ 39% of total PK space\n    let isolation_ratio = divergent.len() as f64 / BUCKET_COUNT as f64;\n    assert!((isolation_ratio - (100.0 / 256.0)).abs() < 0.01);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_bucket_count_constant() {\n    // Verify BUCKET_COUNT
